blocks.normalized_text: sa pagpapatuloy ng mga pagdinig o paglilitis na ito hinihiling namin ang lyong gabay ipagkaloob ninyo sa amin ang mga senator-judges ang karunungan na matukoy ang katotohanan pagpapakumbaba na makinig nang may bukas na isip at lakas ng hob na gawin kung ano ang tama ayon sa aming konsiyensya at ang rule of law
blocks.raw_text: Sa pagpapatuloy ng mga pagdinig o paglilitis na ito, hinihiling namin ang lyong gabay. Ipagkaloob Ninyo sa amin—ang mga senator-judges, ang karunungan na matukoy ang katotohanan, pagpapakumbaba na makinig nang may bukas na isip, at lakas ng hob na gawin kung ano ang tama ayon sa aming konsiyensya at ang rule of law.
blocks.source_text:
Sa pagpapatuloy ng mga pagdinig o paglilitis na ito, hinihiling namin ang lyong 
gabay. Ipagkaloob Ninyo sa amin—ang mga senator-judges, ang karunungan na 
matukoy ang katotohanan, pagpapakumbaba na makinig nang may bukas na isip, 
at lakas ng hob na gawin kung ano ang tama ayon sa aming konsiyensya at ang 
rule of law.

blocks.normalized_text: senator-judge tulfo e reminded the members over the use of the court s time to address personal matters unrelated to the impeachment case stating that such issues should instead be tackled in the senate media briefing room through social media channels or in other forums accordingly he appealed to the presiding officer to strictly enforce the rules by confining the proceedings to matters relevant to the impeachment trial thereby avoiding unnecessary delays in the resolution of the case
blocks.raw_text: Senator-Judge Tulfo (E) reminded the Members over the use of the Court’s time to address personal matters unrelated to the impeachment case, stating that such issues should instead be tackled in the Senate media briefing room, through social media channels, or in other forums. Accordingly, he appealed to the Presiding Officer to strictly enforce the Rules by confining the proceedings to matters relevant to the impeachment trial, thereby avoiding unnecessary delays in the resolution of the case.
blocks.source_text:
Senator-Judge Tulfo (E) reminded the Members over the use of the Court’s time to address 
personal matters unrelated to the impeachment case, stating that such issues should instead be 
tackled in the Senate media briefing room, through social media channels, or in other forums. 
Accordingly, he appealed to the Presiding Officer to strictly enforce the Rules by confining the 
proceedings to matters relevant to the impeachment trial, thereby avoiding unnecessary delays 
in the resolution of the case.

blocks.normalized_text: senator-judge cayetano a asserted that issues affecting the integrity and credibility of the members of the impeachment court are relevant to the proceedings as they bear directly on public confidence in the court he maintained that senator-judge padilla s manifestation merely clarified his compliance with the office of the ombudsman s directive as evidenced by proof of filing through registered mail
blocks.raw_text: Senator-Judge Cayetano (A) asserted that issues affecting the integrity and credibility of the Members of the Impeachment Court are relevant to the proceedings, as they bear directly on public confidence in the Court. He maintained that Senator-Judge Padilla’s manifestation merely clarified his compliance with the Office of the Ombudsman’s directive, as evidenced by proof of filing through registered mail.
blocks.source_text:
Senator-Judge Cayetano (A) asserted that issues affecting the integrity and credibility of 
the Members of the Impeachment Court are relevant to the proceedings, as they bear directly on 
public confidence in the Court. He maintained that Senator-Judge Padilla’s manifestation merely 
clarified his compliance with the Office of the Ombudsman’s directive, as evidenced by proof 
of filing through registered mail.

blocks.normalized_text: manifestation of senator-judge padilla
blocks.raw_text: MANIFESTATION OF SENATOR-JUDGE PADILLA
blocks.source_text: MANIFESTATION OF SENATOR-JUDGE PADILLA

blocks.normalized_text: senator-judge padilla pointed out that the oxford crimson robes worn by the senator-judges symbolize not only the impeachment trial of the vice president but also the constitutional principles of due process and the equal protection of the laws for every filipino he explained that his earlier manifestation was intended to uphold the integrity of the impeachment court and its members emphasizing that no person should be prejudged without due process
blocks.raw_text: Senator-Judge Padilla pointed out that the Oxford crimson robes worn by the senator-judges symbolize not only the impeachment trial of the Vice President but also the constitutional principles of due process and the equal protection of the laws for every Filipino. He explained that his earlier manifestation was intended to uphold the integrity of the Impeachment Court and its Members, emphasizing that no person should be prejudged without due process.
blocks.source_text:
Senator-Judge Padilla pointed out that the Oxford crimson robes worn by the senator-judges 
symbolize not only the impeachment trial of the Vice President but also the constitutional principles 
of due process and the equal protection of the laws for every Filipino. He explained that his earlier 
manifestation was intended to uphold the integrity of the Impeachment Court and its Members, 
emphasizing that no person should be prejudged without due process.

blocks.normalized_text: manifestation of senator-judge pangilinan
blocks.raw_text: MANIFESTATION OF SENATOR-JUDGE PANGILINAN
blocks.source_text: MANIFESTATION OF SENATOR-JUDGE PANGILINAN

blocks.normalized_text: expressing his support to the earlier motion of senator-judge sotto to refer the matter to the senate as a legislative body senator-judge pangilinan recalled that during the estrada impeachment trial then sen rene cayetano had been allowed to make a manifestation regarding a criminal case allegedly being prepared against him he stated that the impeachment court ultimately resolved to refer the matter to the senate as a legislative body thereby establishing a precedent point of order by senator-judge cayetano a
blocks.raw_text: Expressing his support to the earlier motion of Senator-Judge Sotto to refer the matter to the Senate as a legislative body, Senator-Judge Pangilinan recalled that during the Estrada impeachment trial, then Sen. Rene Cayetano had been allowed to make a manifestation regarding a criminal case allegedly being prepared against him. He stated that the Impeachment Court ultimately resolved to refer the matter to the Senate as a legislative body, thereby establishing a precedent. POINT OF ORDER BY SENATOR-JUDGE CAYETANO (A)
blocks.source_text:
Expressing his support to the earlier motion of Senator-Judge Sotto to refer the matter to the 
Senate as a legislative body, Senator-Judge Pangilinan recalled that during the Estrada impeachment 
trial, then Sen. Rene Cayetano had been allowed to make a manifestation regarding a criminal case 
allegedly being prepared against him. He stated that the Impeachment Court ultimately resolved to 
refer the matter to the Senate as a legislative body, thereby establishing a precedent.
POINT OF ORDER BY SENATOR-JUDGE CAYETANO (A)

blocks.normalized_text: senator cayetano a raised a point of order stating that while the presiding officer ruled that the matter would be taken up later rule 6 of the rules of procedure on impeachment trials provides for the suppletory application of the rules of the senate and the revised rules of court whenever applicable he argued that under the senate rules a senator may rise on a question of personal or collective privilege when threatened or prevented from performing official duties he proposed that the matter be discussed during a caucus
blocks.raw_text: Senator Cayetano (A) raised a point of order, stating that while the Presiding Officer ruled that the matter would be taken up later. Rule 6 of the Rules of Procedure on Impeachment Trials provides for the suppletory application of the Rules of the Senate and the Revised Rules of Court whenever applicable. He argued that under the Senate rules, a senator may rise on a question of personal or collective privilege when threatened or prevented from performing official duties. He proposed that the matter be discussed during a caucus.
blocks.source_text:
Senator Cayetano (A) raised a point of order, stating that while the Presiding Officer ruled that 
the matter would be taken up later. Rule 6 of the Rules of Procedure on Impeachment Trials 
provides for the suppletory application of the Rules of the Senate and the Revised Rules of Court 
whenever applicable. He argued that under the Senate rules, a senator may rise on a question 
of personal or collective privilege when threatened or prevented from performing official duties. 
He proposed that the matter be discussed during a caucus.

blocks.normalized_text: ruling of the presiding officer
blocks.raw_text: RULING OF THE PRESIDING OFFICER
blocks.source_text: RULING OF THE PRESIDING OFFICER

blocks.normalized_text: presiding officer escudero ruled that the manifestation of senator-judge padilla shall remain in the records citing as precedent the matter raised by senator-judge pangilinan wherein the manifestation of the former sen rene cayetano during the estrada impeachment trial was not expunged from the records of the impeachment court but also referred to the senate as a legislative body for appropriate action
blocks.raw_text: Presiding Officer Escudero ruled that the manifestation of Senator-Judge Padilla shall remain in the records, citing as precedent the matter raised by Senator-Judge Pangilinan, wherein the manifestation of the former Sen. Rene Cayetano during the Estrada impeachment trial was not expunged from the records of the Impeachment Court but also referred to the Senate as a legislative body for appropriate action.
blocks.source_text:
Presiding Officer Escudero ruled that the manifestation of Senator-Judge Padilla shall remain 
in the records, citing as precedent the matter raised by Senator-Judge Pangilinan, wherein the 
manifestation of the former Sen. Rene Cayetano during the Estrada impeachment trial was not 
expunged from the records of the Impeachment Court but also referred to the Senate as a 
legislative body for appropriate action.

blocks.normalized_text: the presiding officer appealed to the senator-judges to wait for the resumption of senate sessions after the president s state of the nation address at which time the senators would have the proper forum to raise concerns discuss and if necessary investigate allegations or grievances involving matters affecting them
blocks.raw_text: The Presiding Officer appealed to the senator-judges to wait for the resumption of Senate sessions after the President’s State of the Nation Address, at which time the senators would have the proper forum to raise concerns, discuss, and, if necessary, investigate allegations or grievances involving matters affecting them.
blocks.source_text:
The Presiding Officer appealed to the senator-judges to wait for the resumption of Senate 
sessions after the President’s State of the Nation Address, at which time the senators would have 
the proper forum to raise concerns, discuss, and, if necessary, investigate allegations or grievances 
involving matters affecting them.

blocks.normalized_text: senator-judge cayetano a corrected his earlier statement clarifying that the communication was stamped not sent through registered mail he added that in view of the ruling he would no longer pursue his argument on a provision related to rule vi
blocks.raw_text: Senator-Judge Cayetano (A) corrected his earlier statement, clarifying that the communication was stamped, not sent through registered mail. He added that in view of the ruling, he would no longer pursue his argument on a provision related to Rule VI.
blocks.source_text:
Senator-Judge Cayetano (A) corrected his earlier statement, clarifying that the communication 
was stamped, not sent through registered mail. He added that in view of the ruling, he would no 
longer pursue his argument on a provision related to Rule VI.

blocks.raw_text: Senator-Judge Villanueva asked whether the Impeachment Court may grant or deny a motion in part depending on the legal and factual basis, rather than treat the motions as an all-or-nothing proposition, similar to the procedure in regular courts. In reply, the Presiding Officer clarified that the Court would separately decide on the two motions filed by the Prosecution, and would treat each as a single motion, regardless of the number of issues or sub-issues involved. He explained that as a passive body, the Court should not subdivide and separately vote on portions of a party’s motion, as doing so would make it appear that the Court was acting as a party to the case. He stated that the senator-judges may consider all relevant factors in voting and explain their
blocks.source_text:
Senator-Judge Villanueva asked whether the Impeachment Court may grant or deny a motion 
in part depending on the legal and factual basis, rather than treat the motions as an all-or-nothing 
proposition, similar to the procedure in regular courts. In reply, the Presiding Officer clarified that 
the Court would separately decide on the two motions filed by the Prosecution, and would treat 
each as a single motion, regardless of the number of issues or sub-issues involved. He explained 
that as a passive body, the Court should not subdivide and separately vote on portions of a party’s 
motion, as doing so would make it appear that the Court was acting as a party to the case. 
He stated that the senator-judges may consider all relevant factors in voting and explain their

kung dapat po bang /-produce ang hinihinging records; at pangalawa, kung ang mga records 
na iyon ay magiging admissible, at kung anong evidentiary weight ang dapat ibigay sa kanila.

blocks.raw_text: Naisagot na po yata iyan ni dating Presiding Judge Juan Ponce Emile doon po sa Corona impeachment case, and if I may quote just a brief portion fi-om what he said, “I would like to explain that the resolution of this Court was simply to authorize the issuance of a subpoena, and whether those evidence subpoenaed are admissible evidence, given the fact that they apparently appeared in violation of existing laws, is a question that must be resolved in due course.”
blocks.source_text:
Naisagot na po yata iyan ni dating Presiding Judge Juan Ponce Emile doon po sa Corona 
impeachment case, and if I may quote just a brief portion fi-om what he said, “I would like to explain 
that the resolution of this Court was simply to authorize the issuance of a subpoena, and whether 
those evidence subpoenaed are admissible evidence, given the fact that they apparently appeared 
in violation of existing laws, is a question that must be resolved in due course.”

Dahil sa atin, sa ating pananaw, ang isang subpoena—at hindi lang sa ating pananaw, but even under jurisprudence—mayroon pong requirements bago tayo makapag-issue ng subpoena para maging valid iyon subpoena na iyon. At iyon po iyong napag-usapan natin noong nakaraang linggo na definiteness and relevance.
blocks.source_text:
Yes. While we agree with the position of the prosecution na hindi pa naman pinag-uusapan 
ang admissibility sa puntong ito. What we are questioning is really iyong subpoena mismo po. 
Dahil sa atin, sa ating pananaw, ang isang subpoena—at hindi lang sa ating pananaw, but even 
under jurisprudence—mayroon pong requirements bago tayo makapag-issue ng subpoena para 
maging valid iyon subpoena na iyon. At iyon po iyong napag-usapan natin noong nakaraang 
linggo na definiteness and relevance.

necessarily opening of records, una; pangalawa, to be presented in court; at pangatlo, to be 
admitted as evidence by this Court.

blocks.normalized_text: before i go doon sa requirements let me ask the panel of prosecutors si representative diokno doon po sa pananaw ng depensa on alleged fishing expedition just for the guidance of senator-judges this representation would like to clarify one procedural matter we understand that the concern raised by the defense that the subpoena may be overly broad and at the same time we also recognize that the court has a duty naman to determine whether the records being sought are sufficiently relevant and material to the issues before us
blocks.raw_text: Before I go doon sa requirements, let me ask the panel of Prosecutors, si Representative Diokno, doon po sa pananaw ng depensa on alleged fishing expedition. Just for the guidance of senator-judges, this Representation would like to clarify one procedural matter. We understand that the concern raised by the defense, that the subpoena may be overly broad, and at the same time, we also recognize that the Court has a duty naman to determine whether the records being sought are sufficiently relevant and material to the issues before us.
blocks.source_text:
Before I go doon sa requirements, let me ask the panel of Prosecutors, si Representative 
Diokno, doon po sa pananaw ng depensa on alleged fishing expedition. Just for the guidance of 
senator-judges, this Representation would like to clarify one procedural matter. We understand that 
the concern raised by the defense, that the subpoena may be overly broad, and at the same time, 
we also recognize that the Court has a duty naman to determine whether the records being sought 
are sufficiently relevant and material to the issues before us.

blocks.raw_text: So, Representative Diokno, maaari po ba ninyong ipaliwanag kung paano ninyo tiniyak na ang hinihinging mga records ay hindi po simpleng paghahanap lamang ng posibleng ebidensiya o tinatawag na fishing expedition, kundi ito po ay mga partikular na dokumento na sa tingin po ninyo ay mayroon pong malinaw at direktang kaugnayan dito po sa mga Articles of Impeachment?
blocks.source_text:
So, Representative Diokno, maaari po ba ninyong ipaliwanag kung paano ninyo tiniyak na 
ang hinihinging mga records ay hindi po simpleng paghahanap lamang ng posibleng ebidensiya 
o tinatawag na fishing expedition, kundi ito po ay mga partikular na dokumento na sa tingin 
po ninyo ay mayroon pong malinaw at direktang kaugnayan dito po sa mga Articles of

blocks.normalized_text: ang hinihiling po namin sa mga subpoena ay kaugnay sa article ii which involves charges of unexplained wealth kung titingnan po natin iyong mga jurisprudence pagdating sa unexplained wealth klarong-klaro po na sinabi mismo ng korte suprema na kailangan makita iyong buong larawan hindi lamang po ng mga saln na pinayl file ng isang lingkod-bayan kung hindi lahat ng kaniyang kinita both what we would call lawful and if there is illegitimate income
blocks.raw_text: Ang hinihiling po namin sa mga subpoena ay kaugnay sa Article II, which involves charges of unexplained wealth. Kung titingnan po natin iyong mga jurisprudence pagdating sa unexplained wealth, klarong-klaro po na sinabi mismo ng Korte Suprema na kailangan makita iyong buong larawan, hindi lamang po ng mga SALN na pinayl (file) ng isang lingkod-bayan, kung hindi lahat ng kaniyang kinita, both what we would call lawful and, if there is, illegitimate income.
blocks.source_text:
Ang hinihiling po namin sa mga subpoena ay kaugnay sa Article II, which involves charges 
of unexplained wealth. Kung titingnan po natin iyong mga jurisprudence pagdating sa 
unexplained wealth, klarong-klaro po na sinabi mismo ng Korte Suprema na kailangan makita 
iyong buong larawan, hindi lamang po ng mga SALN na pinayl (file) ng isang lingkod-bayan, 
kung hindi lahat ng kaniyang kinita, both what we would call lawful and, if there is, illegitimate 
income.

blocks.normalized_text: ang saln po kasi ay parang snapshot lang po iyan year-end kung ano iyong mga assets liabilities and net worth ng isang government official ang itr din po ay parang short video lang po iyan ng mga transaksiyon ng isang taxpayer sa kabuoan ng taon na iyon makikita lang po natin iyong buong larawan pag nailabas natin ang mga bank records kasi doon po iyong day-to-day na lumabas at pumasok sa isang account ay mabubuo po natin
blocks.raw_text: Ang SALN po kasi ay parang snapshot lang po iyan, year-end, kung ano iyong mga assets, liabilities, and net worth ng isang government official. Ang ITR din po ay parang short video lang po iyan ng mga transaksiyon ng isang taxpayer sa kabuoan ng taon na iyon. Makikita lang po natin iyong buong larawan pag nailabas natin ang mga bank records. Kasi doon po iyong day-to-day na lumabas at pumasok sa isang account ay mabubuo po natin.
blocks.source_text:
Ang SALN po kasi ay parang snapshot lang po iyan, year-end, kung ano iyong mga assets, 
liabilities, and net worth ng isang government official. Ang ITR din po ay parang short video 
lang po iyan ng mga transaksiyon ng isang taxpayer sa kabuoan ng taon na iyon. Makikita lang 
po natin iyong buong larawan pag nailabas natin ang mga bank records. Kasi doon po iyong 
day-to-day na lumabas at pumasok sa isang account ay mabubuo po natin.

blocks.normalized_text: and in fact in the case of dof-rips v5 merencilla ito lang pong portion ang babasahin ko sabing ganoon ng supreme court the failure of the complainant to take into consideration respondent rafael s net worth from the time he entered the government service and the failure to take into consideration all his possible sources of income in relation to his real and personal properties do not support the conclusion that he has unexplained wealth considering his true net worth was not determined ito po ay sabi ng supreme court ombudsman hindi n yo nagawa iyong inyong trabaho kaya ayaw po namin tumulad sa nangyari kay ombudsman dito sa kasong ito we want to see the entire picture
blocks.raw_text: And in fact, in the case of DOF-RIPS V5. Merencilla, ito lang pong portion ang babasahin ko. Sabing ganoon ng Supreme Court: “The failure of the complainant to take into consideration respondent Rafael’s net worth from the time he entered the government service and the failure to take into consideration all his possible sources of income in relation to his real and personal properties do not support the conclusion that he has unexplained wealth considering his true net worth was not determined.” Ito po ay, sabi ng Supreme Court, “Ombudsman, hindi n 'yo nagawa iyong inyong trabaho." Kaya ayaw po namin tumulad sa nangyari kay Ombudsman dito sa kasong ito—we want to see the entire picture.
blocks.source_text:
And in fact, in the case of DOF-RIPS V5. Merencilla, ito lang pong portion ang babasahin 
ko. Sabing ganoon ng Supreme Court: “The failure of the complainant to take into consideration 
respondent Rafael’s net worth from the time he entered the government service and the failure to 
take into consideration all his possible sources of income in relation to his real and personal 
properties do not support the conclusion that he has unexplained wealth considering his true net 
worth was not determined.” Ito po ay, sabi ng Supreme Court, “Ombudsman, hindi n 'yo nagawa 
iyong inyong trabaho." Kaya ayaw po namin tumulad sa nangyari kay Ombudsman dito sa 
kasong ito—we want to see the entire picture.

blocks.raw_text: Kaya po namin hinihingi iyong BIR records, pati iyong mga bank records, and even the Anti- Money Laundering Council records, para makita po natin iyong buong story as far as both the assets, liabilities, and net worth are concerned, and the lawful, and if there is unlawful income, acquired by the respondent.

Kaya po namin hinihingi iyong BIR records, pati iyong mga bank records, and even the Anti-

Sir, binanggit po kanina noong Defense Pane/ iyong requirements which is, “should be 
relevant and specific,” and, kinikilala po natin dito sa Korte na iyong subpoena is an important

blocks.raw_text: Ang subpoena po ang tanging paraan para makuha nitong Hukuman ang mga kinakailangan po natin na datos at impormasyon at dokumento. At hindi naman po siya intrusive dahil tulad ng nabanggit ko po kanina, hindi pa naman ito question of admissibility. Ang kailangan lang ay wa-produce sa Korte iyong mga dokumentong hinihingi. blocks.source_text:
Representative Diokno. Ang subpoena po ang tanging paraan para makuha nitong 
Hukuman ang mga kinakailangan po natin na datos at impormasyon at dokumento. At hindi 
naman po siya intrusive dahil tulad ng nabanggit ko po kanina, hindi pa naman ito question 
of admissibility. Ang kailangan lang ay wa-produce sa Korte iyong mga dokumentong hinihingi. 
The issue of admissibility will come later, and the Defense will have sufficient opportunity to object 
and to oppose.

same time, we/ are also dealing with a request made in the course of a Constitutional proceeding 
before this Impeachment Court, at bilang mga senator-judges, it’s our constitutional duty to try

blocks.source_text:
Mr. Defense Counsel, iyong Section 8(a) of the Anti-Money LaunderingAct, binanggit n ’yo 
po ito last time, which prohibits the disclosure of confidential information. How should this apply 
to records sought through a subpoena issued by the Impeachment Court? Because, as I read. 
Your Honor, Section 8(a), it imposes confidentiality, but it does not expressly create an absolute 
privilege against the subpoena. There is a distinction between an authorized disclosure and 
production/ pursuant to a lawful order of a constitutional body.

blocks.normalized_text: sabi ninyo this is a constitutional body hindi po bal and as we all know there is an old legal maxim that the spring cannot rise over the source ibig sabihin po noon while the impeachment court is a constitutional creation it caimot go up over and above the constitution itself and when we talk about the impeachment court under article xi we cannot read that in isolation we have to also give due regard for all other provisions of the constitution specifically the bill of rights kung saan due process ay isang bagay na nakatatakpo doon at ipinapakita doon ano ba ang ibig sabihin ng due process due process is kasama po kalakip ng due process ay ang bawat nasasakdal ay meron ding pribilehiyo na ma-protect o maproteksiyonan ng mga batas na isinagawa ng mismong kongreso
blocks.raw_text: Sabi ninyo, this is a constitutional body, hindi po bal And, as we all know, there is an old legal maxim that “the spring cannot rise over the source.” Ibig sabihin po noon, while the Impeachment Court is a constitutional creation, it caimot go up over and above the Constitution itself. And when we talk about the Impeachment Court under Article XI, we cannot read that in isolation. We have to also give due regard for all other provisions of the Constitution, specifically the Bill of Rights, kung saan, due process ay isang bagay na nakatatakpo doon at ipinapakita doon. Ano ba ang ibig sabihin ng due process? Due process is, kasama po, kalakip ng due process ay ang bawat nasasakdal ay meron ding pribilehiyo na ma-protect o maproteksiyonan ng mga batas na isinagawa ng mismong Kongreso.
blocks.source_text:
Sabi ninyo, this is a constitutional body, hindi po bal And, as we all know, there is an old 
legal maxim that “the spring cannot rise over the source.” Ibig sabihin po noon, while the 
Impeachment Court is a constitutional creation, it caimot go up over and above the Constitution 
itself. And when we talk about the Impeachment Court under Article XI, we cannot read that in 
isolation. We have to also give due regard for all other provisions of the Constitution, specifically 
the Bill of Rights, kung saan, due process ay isang bagay na nakatatakpo doon at ipinapakita 
doon. Ano ba ang ibig sabihin ng due process? Due process is, kasama po, kalakip ng due 
process ay ang bawat nasasakdal ay meron ding pribilehiyo na ma-protect o maproteksiyonan 
ng mga batas na isinagawa ng mismong Kongreso.

blocks.normalized_text: in the bank secrecy law merong nakasulat doon aaminin natin in cases of impeachment is an exception pagdating po diyan wala po ni isang exception it could have been easy especially since this is a 2021 amendment could have been easy for congress to have cited impeachment as an exception if it did actually wish to do so but it did not your honor and it is our position that if we were to compel and issue a subpoena against these amlc records we re actually compelling the officials to violate this very section of the law and that is why we are saying na pagdating sa 8 a absolute po iyan it does not say that there is any exception if any court processes are actually issued to compel the production of these documents precisely because ang nasa loot po kasi ng mga record ng amlc are intelligence and that s why it s confidential and once amlc determines that they find out that certain transactions are subject to money laundering or should be actually filed in court the end result is the complaint itself and not the raw data and that is why your honors as to the raw data or the information that they received iyan po ay strictly confidential under 8 a
blocks.raw_text: in the Bank Secrecy Law, merong nakasulat doon, aaminin natin, in cases of Impeachment, is an exception. Pagdating po diyan, wala po ni isang exception. It could have been easy, especially since this is a 2021 amendment, could have been easy for Congress to have cited impeachment as an exception, if it did actually wish to do so. But it did not, Your Honor. And it is our position that if we were to compel and issue a subpoena against these AMLC records, we’re actually compelling the officials to violate this very section of the law. And that is why we are saying na pagdating sa 8(a), absolute po iyan, It does not say that there is any exception if any court processes are actually issued to compel the production of these documents, precisely because ang nasa loot po kasi ng mga record ng AMLC are intelligence, and that’s why it’s confidential. And once AMLC determines, that they find out that certain transactions are subject to money laundering or should be actually filed in court, the end result is the complaint itself and not the raw data. And that is why. Your Honors, as to the raw data or the information that they received, iyan po ay strictly confidential under 8(a).
blocks.source_text:
in the Bank Secrecy Law, merong nakasulat doon, aaminin natin, in cases of Impeachment, is an 
exception. Pagdating po diyan, wala po ni isang exception. It could have been easy, especially 
since this is a 2021 amendment, could have been easy for Congress to have cited impeachment as 
an exception, if it did actually wish to do so. But it did not, Your Honor. And it is our position that 
if we were to compel and issue a subpoena against these AMLC records, we’re actually compelling 
the officials to violate this very section of the law. And that is why we are saying na pagdating 
sa 8(a), absolute po iyan, It does not say that there is any exception if any court processes 
are actually issued to compel the production of these documents, precisely because ang nasa loot 
po kasi ng mga record ng AMLC are intelligence, and that’s why it’s confidential. And once AMLC 
determines, that they find out that certain transactions are subject to money laundering or should 
be actually filed in court, the end result is the complaint itself and not the raw data. And that is 
why. Your Honors, as to the raw data or the information that they received, iyan po ay strictly 
confidential under 8(a).

blocks.source_text:
Senator-Judge Villanueva. Thank you for that. Again, I would like to put on record that 
I was trying to look at that particular section. Section 8(a), and I don’t see anything that says this 
Court would not have any prerogative as to whether or not we can subpoena bank records.

blocks.raw_text: So this is the essence, dear colleagues, parties, of the principle of due process. This is ingrained in our Constitution. Totoo po na may karapatan ang gobyerno at obligasyon ng gobyerno patakbuHin nang maayos ang gobyerno. But it’s always with due respect to the rights of every individual Filipino—that is, the right to life, liberty, and property. So this is the essence, dear colleagues, parties, of the principle of due process. This is 
ingrained in our Constitution. Totoo po na may karapatan ang gobyerno at obligasyon ng 
gobyerno patakbuHin nang maayos ang gobyerno. But it’s always with due respect to the rights

iyong right to life, liberty, and property, siguraduhin mo lang na may baseHan ka. ‘Yun lang 
naman ang sabi.

blocks.normalized_text: so in a landmark case of stonehill v diokno 1967 pa po ito karamihan noong mga lawyers hindi pa pinapanganak the importance of due process was highlighted and the supreme court discussed two points general warrants are void sabi ng supreme court a warrant must be issued on probable cause for one specific offense and must particularly describe the thing being seized so kailangan particular ang description doon sa warrant warrants that leave it to the discretion of the officer serving them to decide what to seize or general warrants are outlawed by the constitution because they place the sanctity of the home and privacy at the mercy of prosecutors so sinabi dito na kailangan specific
blocks.raw_text: So, in a landmark case of StoneHill v. Diokno, 1967 pa po ito—karamiHan noong mga lawyers Hindi pa pinapanganak—the importance of due process was highlighted, and the Supreme Court discussed two points: general warrants are void. Sabi ng Supreme Court, “a warrant must be issued on probable cause for one specific offense and must particularly describe the thing being seized.” So, kailangan particular ang description doon sa warrant. “Warrants that leave it to the discretion of the officer serving them to decide what to seize, or general warrants are outlawed by the Constitution because they place the sanctity of the home and privacy at the mercy of prosecutors.” So sinabi dito na kailangan specific.
blocks.source_text:
So, in a landmark case of StoneHill v. Diokno, 1967 pa po ito—karamiHan noong mga lawyers 
Hindi pa pinapanganak—the importance of due process was highlighted, and the Supreme Court 
discussed two points: general warrants are void. Sabi ng Supreme Court, “a warrant must be issued 
on probable cause for one specific offense and must particularly describe the thing being seized.” 
So, kailangan particular ang description doon sa warrant. “Warrants that leave it to the discretion 
of the officer serving them to decide what to seize, or general warrants are outlawed by the 
Constitution because they place the sanctity of the home and privacy at the mercy of prosecutors.” 
So sinabi dito na kailangan specific.

blocks.normalized_text: in the case of stonehill v diokno the father of congressman diokno was the secretary of justice and in this case the government through the secretary and other prosecutors suspected that harry stonehill and his associates and several corporations that they controlled were violating central bank laws tariff and customs laws the internal revenue code and the revised penal code so nag-request sila ng issuance of a search warrant at ang gusto nilang tingnan is books of accounts financial records vouchers receipts ledgers et cetera and other documents showing all the business transaetions
blocks.raw_text: In the case of Stonehill v. Diokno—the father of Congressman Diokno was the Secretary of Justice—and in this case, the government, through the Secretary and other prosecutors, suspected that Harry Stonehill and his associates, and several corporations that they controlled, were violating central bank laws, tariff and customs laws, the Internal Revenue Code, and the Revised Penal Code. So, nag-request sila ng issuance of a search warrant at ang gusto nilang tingnan is books of accounts, financial records, vouchers, receipts, ledgers, et cetera, and other documents showing all the business transaetions.
blocks.source_text:
Senator-Judge Cayetano (P). In the case of Stonehill v. Diokno—the father of Congressman 
Diokno was the Secretary of Justice—and in this case, the government, through the Secretary and 
other prosecutors, suspected that Harry Stonehill and his associates, and several corporations that 
they controlled, were violating central bank laws, tariff and customs laws, the Internal Revenue 
Code, and the Revised Penal Code. So, nag-request sila ng issuance of a search warrant at ang 
gusto nilang tingnan is books of accounts, financial records, vouchers, receipts, ledgers, et cetera, 
and other documents showing all the business transaetions.

blocks.normalized_text: nandoon dahil very invasive po ang search warrant kapag ang isang judge ay nag-issue ng search warrant puwedeng pasukin iyong opisina o iyong bahay ng person involved puwedeng halungkatin iyong lahat ng mga gamit niya doon sa kanyang opisina o sa kanyang bahay kaya po naglagay ng maraming limitasyon ang ating konstitusyon dahil marami nang pang-aabuso marami nang mga halimbawa ng pang-aabuso na nangyari kahit noon pa iba po ang pinag-uusapan natin ngayon ang pinag-uusapan natin ngayon ay subpoena hindi po ito license to enter an office or to search any place ito po ay mismong humihiling lang ng mga dokumentong nakasulat sa loob ng subpoena therefore the contexts are very different when we speak of a search warrant i do agree there should be very specific limitations on how that warrant can be enforced pagdating naman po sa subpoena mayroon din po iyan but that is not governed by the constitution it s governed by rule 21 of the rules of civil procedure which basically provides that for a subpoena to be valid it must be relevant and it must be definite ibig sabihin po sa relevance ay may kinalaman o may kaugnayan iyan sa mga kailangan patunayan na mga facts at pagdating naman sa definiteness ay klaro kung anong mga iba t ibang dokumento ang hinihingi
blocks.raw_text: nandoon, dahil very invasive po ang search warrant. Kapag ang isang judge ay nag-issue ng search warrant, puwedeng pasukin iyong opisina o iyong bahay ng person involved, puwedeng halungkatin iyong lahat ng mga gamit niya doon sa kanyang opisina o sa kanyang bahay. Kaya po naglagay ng maraming limitasyon ang ating Konstitusyon dahil marami nang pang-aabuso, marami nang mga halimbawa ng pang-aabuso na nangyari kahit noon pa. Iba po ang pinag- uusapan natin ngayon. Ang pinag-uusapan natin ngayon ay subpoena. Hindi po ito license to enter an office or to search any place. Ito po ay mismong humihiling lang ng mga dokumentong nakasulat sa loob ng subpoena. Therefore, the contexts are very different. When we speak of a search warrant, I do agree there should be very specific limitations on how that warrant can be enforced. Pagdating naman po sa subpoena, mayroon din po iyan, but that is not governed by the Constitution. It’s governed by Rule 21 of the Rules of Civil Procedure, which basically provides that for a subpoena to be valid, it must be relevant and it must be definite. Ibig sabihin po sa relevance ay may kinalaman, o may kaugnayan iyan sa mga kailangan patunayan na mga facts, at pagdating naman sa definiteness ay klaro kung anong mga iba't ibang dokumento ang hinihingi.

blocks.source_text:
In our case, we respectfully submit. Your Honors, that we have met both the standards of 
relevance and materiality. The BIR records, the bank records, and the records of the Anti-Money 
Laimdering Council are relevant to the facts in issue pertaining to the charges of unexplained 
wealth. Hindi po natin malalaman kung talagang mayroong unexplained wealth ang Vice 
President kung hindi natin mabubuksan iyong kanyang mga bank records, kung hindi rin natin 
makikita at mailalatag ang Anti-Money Laundering at BIR records po nila. And insofar as 
definiteness is concerned, we have specified the particular documents that we are requesting, and 
we have included as well an explanation of why those are important. Kaya sa aming pananaw po 
ay we have satisfied the requirements for a subpoena duces tecum.

blocks.normalized_text: iyon pong search warrant kung ano man ang makuhang ebidensiya doon ay direktang binibigay po iyon sa court and that is now considered as evidence by the court that issued the search warrant iba po ang subpoena dahil kapag naglabas ang isang hukuman ng subpoena itong mga records ay hindi po siya considered yet as evidence lyan po ay ipo-produce lang dadalhin lang po sa korte and now it is up to the parties and i refer to both parties to decide which of those documents may be relevant in terms of the evidence that they need to present iyon pong nakikita po natin sa subpoena it is not in any way invasive because as i mentioned earlier and as pointed out by senator enrile when he was the presiding officer in the corona trial we are simply having those documents produced the admissibility of those documents will only be determined after if offered by the parties kaya po sa aming mga pananaw ay malaking-malaki ang pagkakaiba ng search warrant sa subpoena
blocks.raw_text: Iyon pong search warrant, kung ano man ang makuhang ebidensiya doon ay direktang binibigay po iyon sa court, and that is now considered as evidence by the court that issued the search warrant. Iba po ang subpoena, dahil kapag naglabas ang isang hukuman ng subpoena, itong mga records ay hindi po siya considered yet as evidence. lyan po ay ipo- produce lang, dadalhin lang po sa korte, and now it is up to the parties, and I refer to both parties, to decide which of those documents may be relevant in terms of the evidence that they need to present. Iyon pong nakikita po natin sa subpoena, it is not in any way invasive. Because, as I mentioned earlier, and as pointed out by Senator Enrile when he was the presiding officer in the Corona trial, we are simply having those documents produced. The admissibility of those documents will only be determined after, if offered by the parties. Kaya po, sa aming mga pananaw, ay malaking-malaki ang pagkakaiba ng search warrant sa subpoena.
blocks.source_text:
Representative Diokno. Iyon pong search warrant, kung ano man ang makuhang ebidensiya 
doon ay direktang binibigay po iyon sa court, and that is now considered as evidence by the court 
that issued the search warrant. Iba po ang subpoena, dahil kapag naglabas ang isang hukuman 
ng subpoena, itong mga records ay hindi po siya considered yet as evidence. lyan po ay ipo- 
produce lang, dadalhin lang po sa korte, and now it is up to the parties, and I refer to both parties, 
to decide which of those documents may be relevant in terms of the evidence that they need to 
present. Iyon pong nakikita po natin sa subpoena, it is not in any way invasive. Because, as I 
mentioned earlier, and as pointed out by Senator Enrile when he was the presiding officer in the 
Corona trial, we are simply having those documents produced. The admissibility of those 
documents will only be determined after, if offered by the parties. Kaya po, sa aming mga 
pananaw, ay malaking-malaki ang pagkakaiba ng search warrant sa subpoena.

blocks.raw_text: Your Honors, ang posisyon po ng Depensa dito ay subpoena and a search warrant are very much similar, and both are compulsory processes. So, yes, hindi tayo papasok sa bahay ng isang tao o ng nagsasakdal, but it will be intrusive because we will be opening up all the financial transactions for—sabi nga dito— almost 20 years of the Respondent. Kaya po, katulad ng isang search warrant, the things that we are asking for through a subpoena must be particularly described. Kanina po, nabanggit po ng aking kagalang-galang na Opposing Counsel na particularly described na po iyong kanilang naisulat. However, the Defense does not agree with that proposition because if you look at it, tama naman po, nakasulat po dito na ang hinihingi nila ay account opening and maintenance records, KYC documents, information files, signature cards, all account and transaction records, ledgers, journals. Basically, Your Honors, this is everything. Ibig sabihin, /«/-specify lang nila ang nilalaman ng lahat ng bank records ng Respondent. So, in our view. Your Honors, while they took pains to specify each and every document, the truth of the matter is, they are asking for everything. Ngayon, pagdating sa subpoena, and even in the case they cited, iyong Roco v. Contreras, nakasulat po doon that for a subpoena to be validly issued—when we talk about particularly describing kung ano iyong hinihingi—iit has to be evidenced that is both relevant and material. So, ang tanong namin, saan ba dito iyong relevant or material? Kasi lahat na po ng records hiningi. So—and this bolsters our theory, of course, with all due respect to the opposing side, na kaya namin tinatawag o kaya namin natawag na fishing expedition ito, kasi lahat na ng records hiningi, pero hindi naman matukoy which would actually be relevant, which would actually be evidentiary to the accusations they’ve made under the Articles of Impeachment. So, pero dito, hinihingi natin 20 years’ worth of records, and then papaano po iyon hihimayin natin? Maghahanap tayo ngayon kung may relevant o wala? Which brings me back to iyong BIR records. Naka-seal po iyong box. So, I would

blocks.normalized_text: i just wanted to point out that this is the first time actually an impeachment court is faced with the challenge of determining whether the charges of unexplained wealth are valid or not ang previous corona case hindi po pinayagan ng impeachment court iyong article 2 4 ng article ii at kung ang magiging batayan po natin ay iyong naging mga desisyon ng ating korte suprema sinabi mismo ng supreme court in several cases na kapag ang usapin ay unexplained wealth kailangan makita iyong kabuuan ng kinita ng isang lingkod bayan at iyong mga dineclare niya sa saln just to quote just a very short portion sabi ng supreme court from the same case cited earlier the ombudsman whose failure to account for all lawful sources of income including proceeds from business ventures and government benefits further weakens the allegation that rafael s wealth was unexplained the incomplete assessment disregarded his accumulated assets over decades of public service making it impossible to determine whether his property acquisitions exceed his legitimate financial capacity hindi lang naman po dito sa harapan nitong hukom na ginagawa iyong pagtingin sa kabuuan ng isang lingkod bayan the supreme court has done it in this case where they looked at decades of the financial records of the person charged with unexplained wealth and it has also happened in the cases of rabusa and ligot which were decided recently by the honorable court thank you
blocks.raw_text: I just wanted to point out that this is the first time actually an Impeachment Court is faced with the challenge of determining whether the charges of unexplained wealth are valid or not. Ang previous Corona case, hindi po pinayagan ng Impeachment Court iyong Article 2.4 ng Article II, at kung ang magiging batayan po natin ay iyong naging mga desisyon ng ating Korte Suprema, sinabi mismo ng Supreme Court in several cases na kapag ang usapin ay unexplained wealth, kailangan makita iyong kabuuan ng kinita ng isang lingkod bayan at iyong mga dineclare niya sa SALN. Just to quote, just a very short portion, sabi ng Supreme Court, from the same case cited earlier, “The Ombudsman whose failure to account for all lawful sources of income including proceeds from business ventures and government benefits further weakens the allegation that Rafael’s wealth was unexplained. The incomplete assessment disregarded his accumulated assets over decades of public service, making it impossible to determine whether his property acquisitions exceed his legitimate financial capacity.” Hindi lang naman po dito sa harapan nitong Hukom na ginagawa iyong pagtingin sa kabuuan ng isang lingkod bayan. The Supreme Court has done it in this case where they looked at decades of the financial records of the person charged with unexplained wealth, and it has also happened in the cases of Rabusa and Ligot, which were decided recently by the Honorable Court. Thank you.
blocks.source_text:
I just wanted to point out that this is the first time actually an Impeachment Court is faced 
with the challenge of determining whether the charges of unexplained wealth are valid or not. Ang 
previous Corona case, hindi po pinayagan ng Impeachment Court iyong Article 2.4 ng Article II, 
at kung ang magiging batayan po natin ay iyong naging mga desisyon ng ating Korte Suprema, 
sinabi mismo ng Supreme Court in several cases na kapag ang usapin ay unexplained wealth, 
kailangan makita iyong kabuuan ng kinita ng isang lingkod bayan at iyong mga dineclare niya 
sa SALN. Just to quote, just a very short portion, sabi ng Supreme Court, from the same case cited 
earlier, “The Ombudsman whose failure to account for all lawful sources of income including 
proceeds from business ventures and government benefits further weakens the allegation that 
Rafael’s wealth was unexplained. The incomplete assessment disregarded his accumulated assets 
over decades of public service, making it impossible to determine whether his property acquisitions 
exceed his legitimate financial capacity.” Hindi lang naman po dito sa harapan nitong Hukom na 
ginagawa iyong pagtingin sa kabuuan ng isang lingkod bayan. The Supreme Court has done 
it in this case where they looked at decades of the financial records of the person charged with 
unexplained wealth, and it has also happened in the cases of Rabusa and Ligot, which were decided 
recently by the Honorable Court. Thank you.

blocks.raw_text: My next point, Mr. Presiding Officer and parties, is still, is still on due process. This is the requirement that every pleading must allege ultimate facts and must not contain conclusions of law. So, again. I’ll start with an example. If, for example, if there is a case filed, ‘no, and, sabi, si “Juan one robbed the house of Mario.’’ So, para maintindihan ng mga kababayan natin,”iwz.n robbed the house of Mario.” That is a conclusion of fact, nangyari na daw’ yun. And if there’s another allegation that says, therefore, Juan is guilty of robbery, that is a conclusion of law. So, again, I try my best not to be technical. It’s in my—I would have wanted to be a teacher if not a lawyer, so, I do like to be able to explain things well. So, ang hinahanap dito is bakit mo—gusto mong patunayan ang facts mo. Iyon ang kailangan sa ultimate facts. Kailangan mong patunayan bakit mo sinasabing “Juan robbed the house of Mario.” Sasabihin mo ang ultimate facts diyan, example, is, si Juan ay nakita doon sa vicinity ng bahay ni Mario, tapos itong si Mario, narinig na nagsisigaw na ninanakawan kami, whatever. Tapos, mayroon siyang testimonya na ano iyong nawala sa kanila, tapos may nakakita na kamukha ni Juan iyong nandoon, tapos namukhaan nila, na-describe nila na 5 feet 10, medyo mapayat, ganoon. So, iyon ang mga ultimate facts na later on sa evidence, patutunayan. It’s just very general. And then later on, para nga mapatunayan iyong robbery, i-a-allege ngayon iyong elements of the crime that constitute robbery. So, noong hindi pa ako abogado, hindi ko naman alam ang difference ng robbery and theft. Iyong robbery kasi qualified iyan. So, pumasok siya sa bahay using violence. That’s what makes it robbery. So, kailangan /-allege iyon. Sorry to be a bit technical, alam kong iyan ang phrase of the day, let’s not be technical, phrase of the week, phrase of the month, but we have to be able to explain these things in a way that people understand, without forgetting that these are our rights of the defense and rights of the person who is being tried here, and kailangan maliwanag iyong ating mga accusation.

My next point, Mr. Presiding Officer and parties, is still, is still on due process. This is the 
requirement that every pleading must allege ultimate facts and must not contain conclusions of law. 
So, again. I’ll start with an example. If, for example, if there is a case filed, ‘no, and, sabi, si “Juan 
one robbed the house of Mario.’’ So, para maintindihan ng mga kababayan natin,”iwz.n robbed 
the house of Mario.” That is a conclusion of fact, nangyari na daw’ yun. And if there’s another 
allegation that says, therefore, Juan is guilty of robbery, that is a conclusion of law. So, again, 
I try my best not to be technical. It’s in my—I would have wanted to be a teacher if not a lawyer, 
so, I do like to be able to explain things well. So, ang hinahanap dito is bakit mo—gusto mong 
patunayan ang facts mo. Iyon ang kailangan sa ultimate facts. Kailangan mong patunayan bakit 
mo sinasabing “Juan robbed the house of Mario.” Sasabihin mo ang ultimate facts diyan, example, 
is, si Juan ay nakita doon sa vicinity ng bahay ni Mario, tapos itong si Mario, narinig na 
nagsisigaw na ninanakawan kami, whatever. Tapos, mayroon siyang testimonya na ano iyong 
nawala sa kanila, tapos may nakakita na kamukha ni Juan iyong nandoon, tapos namukhaan 
nila, na-describe nila na 5 feet 10, medyo mapayat, ganoon. So, iyon ang mga ultimate facts na 
later on sa evidence, patutunayan. It’s just very general. And then later on, para nga 
mapatunayan iyong robbery, i-a-allege ngayon iyong elements of the crime that constitute 
robbery. So, noong hindi pa ako abogado, hindi ko naman alam ang difference ng robbery and 
theft. Iyong robbery kasi qualified iyan. So, pumasok siya sa bahay using violence. That’s what 
makes it robbery. So, kailangan /-allege iyon. Sorry to be a bit technical, alam kong iyan ang 
phrase of the day, let’s not be technical, phrase of the week, phrase of the month, but we have 
to be able to explain these things in a way that people understand, without forgetting that these 
are our rights of the defense and rights of the person who is being tried here, and kailangan 
maliwanag iyong ating mga accusation.

and truthfully disclose all her and her spouse’s assets, liabilities, and net worth in her Statement

blocks.raw_text: Please allow me to briefly explain why these are ultimate facts. Ultimate facts are basically the essential factual elements that make up a party’s cause of action or defense. Halimbawa po, pag murder, treachery, evident premeditation, lahat ng mga kinakailangan ng batas para mapatunayan na murder po siya. In our case, we are alleging, first of all, unexplained wealth. Please allow me to briefly explain why these are ultimate facts. Ultimate facts are basically 
the essential factual elements that make up a party’s cause of action or defense. Halimbawa po, 
pag murder, treachery, evident premeditation, lahat ng mga kinakailangan ng batas para 
mapatunayan na murder po siya. In our case, we are alleging, first of all, unexplained wealth. And 
based on the jurisprudence, the elements of unexplained wealth—the factual elements—are 
amassing unexplained wealth manifestly disproportionate to one’s lawful income and earnings 
during one’s incumbency as a public official.

Second po, ang fishing expedition maaaring magamit iyan sa ibang mga alegasyon tulad ng, 
baka meron kang hindi na-declare sa iyong SALN. But when it comes to issues of unexplained 
wealth, it is very clear—the decisions of the court are very clear—to know whether a government 
official has amassed unexplained wealth, we must look at the entire incumbency of that official. 
Starting from day one, we look at their SALN, we look at all the income that he or she has earned, 
both lawful and not, and from there we make a determination whether there was unexplained wealth 
or not.

Mr. Diokno. Your Honor, Section 8(a) of the Anti-Money Laundering Law is really 
intended to prohibit or prevent leaks by officials of the Anti-Money Laundering Council, 
but it was never intended to prevent subpoenas coming from courts, including the impeachment 
court.

blocks.source_text:
Almost every week, the Sandiganbayan issues subpoenas for AMLC records. And despite 
what would appear to be a very categorical prohibition contained in that section, every time 
subpoenas are issued by the Sandiganbayan, they are honored by the AMLC, and no one is 
questioning them.

blocks.normalized_text: and again this was the latest amendment of the law back in 2021 and for us your honors that is why when this was presented to the house committee on justice we had our objections to such matter and in fact your honors it is a subject of our petition in the supreme court and aside from that a subject of our motion for exclusion before this honorable court ngayon po sa atin talagang yung tinutumbok natin is it is in fact within the fruit of the poisonous tree in fact i m surprised your honors because your honors nabanggit na rin po ng aking distinguished opposing counsel na nakita na nila may records na sila in fact if i m not mistaken it was also attached to the articles of impeachment so kung meron na pala bakit pa nila pmqpa-subpoena ngayon sa impeachment court is it because i don t want to speculate maybe i just won t go there but ang sa akin lang po is we have questioned this all throughout
blocks.raw_text: And, again, this was the latest amendment of the law back in 2021. And, for us. Your Honors, that is why when this was presented to the House Committee on Justice, we had our objections to such matter. And, in fact. Your Honors, it is a subject of our petition in the Supreme Court and, aside from that, a subject of our Motion for Exclusion before this Honorable Court. Ngayon po, sa atin, talagang ‘yung tinutumbok natin is it is, in fact, within the fruit of the poisonous tree. In fact. I’m surprised. Your Honors, because. Your Honors, nabanggit na rin po ng aking distinguished Opposing Counsel na nakita na nila, may records na sila. In fact, if I’m not mistaken, it was also attached to the Articles of Impeachment. So, kung meron na pala, bakit pa nila pmqpa-subpoena ngayon sa Impeachment Court? Is it because—I don’t want to speculate, maybe I just won’t go there. But, ang sa akin lang po is, we have questioned this all throughout.
blocks.source_text:
And, again, this was the latest amendment of the law back in 2021. And, for us. Your Honors, 
that is why when this was presented to the House Committee on Justice, we had our objections 
to such matter. And, in fact. Your Honors, it is a subject of our petition in the Supreme Court and, 
aside from that, a subject of our Motion for Exclusion before this Honorable Court. Ngayon po, 
sa atin, talagang ‘yung tinutumbok natin is it is, in fact, within the fruit of the poisonous tree.
In fact. I’m surprised. Your Honors, because. Your Honors, nabanggit na rin po ng aking 
distinguished Opposing Counsel na nakita na nila, may records na sila. In fact, if I’m not 
mistaken, it was also attached to the Articles of Impeachment. So, kung meron na pala, bakit pa 
nila pmqpa-subpoena ngayon sa Impeachment Court? Is it because—I don’t want to speculate, 
maybe I just won’t go there. But, ang sa akin lang po is, we have questioned this all throughout.

blocks.normalized_text: why because to us unang-una bawal siya pangalawa hindi siya relevant bakit hindi siya relevant your honors transactions do not equate to wealth transactions do not equate to net worth especially if tinatali natin siya sa saln because transactions happen over the course of the year and in this case years well when you talk about saln we talk about net worth on a specific date and time which is december 31 of every year so sa atin po ang aming pananaw dito ay hindi po relevant at it will not directly show anything about wealth kasi again amlc transactions do not equate to net worth of a certain person and that is why your honors we have opposed this and we have opposed this since the time it was being presented at the house level
blocks.raw_text: Why? Because to us, unang-una, bawal siya. Pangalawa, hindi siya relevant. Bakit hindi siya relevant? Your Honors, transactions do not equate to wealth. Transactions do not equate to net worth, especially if tinatali natin siya sa SALN. Because transactions happen over the course of the year and, in this case, years. Well, when you talk about SALN, we talk about net worth on a specific date and time, which is December 31 of every year. So, sa atin po, ang aming pananaw dito ay hindi po relevant at it will not directly show anything about wealth. Kasi, again, AMLC transactions do not equate to net worth of a certain person. And that is why. Your Honors, we have opposed this, and we have opposed this since the time it was being presented at the House level.
blocks.source_text:
Why? Because to us, unang-una, bawal siya. Pangalawa, hindi siya relevant. Bakit hindi 
siya relevant? Your Honors, transactions do not equate to wealth. Transactions do not equate 
to net worth, especially if tinatali natin siya sa SALN. Because transactions happen over the 
course of the year and, in this case, years. Well, when you talk about SALN, we talk about net 
worth on a specific date and time, which is December 31 of every year. So, sa atin po, ang aming 
pananaw dito ay hindi po relevant at it will not directly show anything about wealth. Kasi, again, 
AMLC transactions do not equate to net worth of a certain person. And that is why. Your Honors, 
we have opposed this, and we have opposed this since the time it was being presented at the 
House level.

blocks.normalized_text: so counsel may i ask let s start with the case of duterte v house of representatives thank you for reminding us about certain precedents and how i voted last time but you would agree di ba that when you have legal proceedings even in legislation you follow what the latest law is so for example if you re allowed to issue a general warrant before stonehill v diokno but after stonehill v diokno may so if before stonehill v diokno walang doctrine of the fruit of the poisonous tree then some policemen will still submit evidence that was illegally acquired but after that we have to follow so you did make your point about obiter dictum i ll go to that but i just want to start out by saying the fact is there is a duterte v house of representatives so with your permission let s start discussing that
blocks.raw_text: So, Counsel, may I ask—let’s start with the case of Duterte v. House of Representatives. Thank you for reminding us about certain precedents and how I voted last time. But you would agree, 'di ba, that when you have legal proceedings, even in legislation, you follow what the latest law is. So, for example, if you’re allowed to issue a general warrant before Stonehill v. Diokno, but after Stonehill v. Diokno, may—so, if before Stonehill v. Diokno, walang doctrine of the fruit of the poisonous tree, then some policemen will still submit evidence that was illegally acquired. But after that, we have to follow. So, you did make your point about obiter dictum. I’ll go to that, but I just want to start out by saying the fact is there is a Duterte v. House of Representatives. So, with your permission, let’s start discussing that.
blocks.source_text:
So, Counsel, may I ask—let’s start with the case of Duterte v. House of Representatives. Thank 
you for reminding us about certain precedents and how I voted last time. But you would agree,
'di ba, that when you have legal proceedings, even in legislation, you follow what the latest law 
is. So, for example, if you’re allowed to issue a general warrant before Stonehill v. Diokno, but after 
Stonehill v. Diokno, may—so, if before Stonehill v. Diokno, walang doctrine of the fruit of the 
poisonous tree, then some policemen will still submit evidence that was illegally acquired. But after 
that, we have to follow. So, you did make your point about obiter dictum. I’ll go to that, but I just 
want to start out by saying the fact is there is a Duterte v. House of Representatives. So, with your 
permission, let’s start discussing that.

blocks.raw_text: Well, I would distinguish, Your Honor, please, between what we would call ratio decidendi, or the rule in that particular case, and obiter dicta, which are side comments or incidental remarks made in the opinion of the Court. blocks.normalized_text: but in villanueva v court of appeals in that case the supreme court held an adjudication on any point within the issues presented by the case cannot be considered an obiter dictum even where the point is only incidentally involved so we agree if it s incidental puwedeng obiter but if it s on point within the issues sabi ng villanueva case it is not so long as it is presented and decided in the regular course of the consideration of the case and led up to the final conclusion or is a statement as to the matter of which decision is predicated
blocks.raw_text: But in Villanueva v. Court of Appeals, in that case, the Supreme Court held: “An adjudication on any point within the issues presented by the case cannot be considered an obiter dictum, even where the point is only incidentally involved.” So, we agree if it’s incidental, puwedeng obiter, but if it’s on point within the issues—sabi ng Villanueva case, it is not, so long as it is presented and decided in the regular course of the consideration of the case and led up to the final conclusion, or is a statement as to the matter of which decision is predicated.
blocks.source_text:
But in Villanueva v. Court of Appeals, in that case, the Supreme Court held: “An adjudication 
on any point within the issues presented by the case cannot be considered an obiter dictum, even 
where the point is only incidentally involved.” So, we agree if it’s incidental, puwedeng obiter, but 
if it’s on point within the issues—sabi ng Villanueva case, it is not, so long as it is presented and 
decided in the regular course of the consideration of the case and led up to the final conclusion, 
or is a statement as to the matter of which decision is predicated.

blocks.normalized_text: the court added that a point expressly decided does not lose its value as a precedent because the disposition of the case is or might have been made on some other ground and that where a court decides several points none of such points can t be regarded as having the status of a dictum and one point should not be denied authority merely because another point was and more dwelt on and more fully argued and considered
blocks.raw_text: The Court added that a point expressly decided does not lose its value as a precedent because the disposition of the case is or might have been made on some other ground, and that where a court decides several points, none of such points can’t be regarded as having the status of a dictum, and one point should not be denied authority merely because another point was—and more dwelt on and more fully argued—and considered.
blocks.source_text:
The Court added that a point expressly decided does not lose its value as a precedent because 
the disposition of the case is or might have been made on some other ground, and that where a 
court decides several points, none of such points can’t be regarded as having the status of a 
dictum, and one point should not be denied authority merely because another point was—and more 
dwelt on and more fully argued—and considered.

blocks.normalized_text: so going back to the duterte v house of representatives the requirement that an impeachable act be committed in relation to their office and during the current term was not a remark made by by the way the supreme court en banc laid it down as one of the enumerated principles that due process requires which is the very ground on which the ruling was predicated even if the court also struck down the 2025 impeachment complaint based on the one-year bar so this is the question does it not render the due process requirement laid out as a mere obiter dicta or because it s based on due process regardless of the mode of initiation regardless of the mode of how the articles of impeachment got here dsn t due process still a requirement
blocks.raw_text: So, going back to the Duterte v. House of Representatives, the requirement that an impeachable act be committed in relation to their office and during the current term was not a remark made by “by the way.” The Supreme Court en banc laid it down as one of the enumerated principles that due process requires, which is the very ground on which the ruling was predicated. Even if the Court also struck down the 2025 impeachment complaint based on the one-year bar, so this is the question: Does it not render the due process requirement laid out as a mere obiter dicta, or because it’s based on due process, regardless of the mode of initiation—regardless of the mode of how the Articles of Impeachment got here. Dsn’t due process still a requirement?

blocks.normalized_text: precisely your honor if a matter is within the issues resolved by the court then it may not be considered as obiter dicta but if we look at the decision in the case of duterte v house of representatives ang mismong nangyari po roon ay may naunang dalawang complaints that were endorsed at may sumunod na complaint filed by at least one-third of the members of the house at iyon ang tinutukan po ng ating supreme court it was in that context that the supreme court made pronouncements about the one-year bar and even about due process at no time in that case was it ever a fact and issue about the mode of impeachment that is involved in our case today ang pinag-uusapan po natin dito ay individual complaints that were then referred by the house to the committee on justice heard by the committee and then brought back to the plenary and eventually umabot po rito through articles of impeachment so kahit may sinabi ang supreme court about impeachable offenses in relation to the charges kami po ay naniniwala na hindi po yan pumapasok o sumasakop sa issues that were decided in that case
blocks.raw_text: Precisely, Your Honor. If a matter is within the issues resolved by the Court, then it may not be considered as obiter dicta. But if we look at the decision in the case of Duterte v. House of Representatives, ang mismong nangyari po roon ay may naunang dalawang complaints that were endorsed, at may sumunod na complaint filed by at least one-third of the Members of the House, at iyon ang tinutukan po ng ating Supreme Court. It was in that context that the Supreme Court made pronouncements about the one-year bar and even about due process. At no time in that case was it ever a fact and issue about the mode of impeachment that is involved in our case today. Ang pinag-uusapan po natin dito ay individual complaints that were then referred by the House to the Committee on Justice, heard by the committee, and then brought back to the Plenary, and eventually umabot po rito, through Articles of Impeachment. So, kahit may sinabi ang Supreme Court about impeachable offenses in relation to the charges, kami po ay naniniwala na hindi po ‘yan pumapasok o sumasakop sa issues that were decided in that case.
blocks.source_text:
Representative Diokno. Precisely, Your Honor. If a matter is within the issues resolved by the 
Court, then it may not be considered as obiter dicta. But if we look at the decision in the case 
of Duterte v. House of Representatives, ang mismong nangyari po roon ay may naunang 
dalawang complaints that were endorsed, at may sumunod na complaint filed by at least one-third 
of the Members of the House, at iyon ang tinutukan po ng ating Supreme Court. It was in that 
context that the Supreme Court made pronouncements about the one-year bar and even about due 
process. At no time in that case was it ever a fact and issue about the mode of impeachment that 
is involved in our case today. Ang pinag-uusapan po natin dito ay individual complaints that were 
then referred by the House to the Committee on Justice, heard by the committee, and then brought 
back to the Plenary, and eventually umabot po rito, through Articles of Impeachment. So, kahit 
may sinabi ang Supreme Court about impeachable offenses in relation to the charges, kami po ay 
naniniwala na hindi po ‘yan pumapasok o sumasakop sa issues that were decided in that case.

blocks.normalized_text: first of all if your honor please when we speak of due process of course there are two types substantive and procedural due process and when we speak of substantive we re talking about protections to life liberty and property wala naman pong ganoon na issue po rito sa atin because office is not a property we re not talking about the life or liberty of the respondent ang pinag-uusapan po natin dito kung due process ang issue ay simply fundamental fairness and i do believe that this proceeding does require fundamental fairness but at the same time it does not require that this court be hamstrung in terms of how to decide whether an offense is impeachable or not that power is exclusively given to this court at dapat kayo lang po ang magdesisyon at magpasya kung ano nga ba yan
blocks.raw_text: First of all, if Your Honor, please, when we speak of due process, of course, there are two types: substantive and procedural due process. And when we speak of substantive, we’re talking about protections to life, liberty, and property. Wala naman pong ganoon na issue po rito sa atin, because office is not a property. We’re not talking about the life or liberty of the Respondent. Ang pinag-uusapan po natin dito, kung due process ang issue, ay simply fundamental fairness. And I do believe that this proceeding does require fundamental fairness, but at the same time, it does not require that this Court be hamstrung in terms of how to decide whether an offense is impeachable or not. That power is exclusively given to this Court, at dapat kayo lang po ang magdesisyon at magpasya kung ano nga ba 'yan.
blocks.source_text:
Representative Diokno. First of all, if Your Honor, please, when we speak of due process, of 
course, there are two types: substantive and procedural due process. And when we speak of 
substantive, we’re talking about protections to life, liberty, and property. Wala naman pong 
ganoon na issue po rito sa atin, because office is not a property. We’re not talking about the life 
or liberty of the Respondent. Ang pinag-uusapan po natin dito, kung due process ang issue, ay 
simply fundamental fairness. And I do believe that this proceeding does require fundamental 
fairness, but at the same time, it does not require that this Court be hamstrung in terms of how 
to decide whether an offense is impeachable or not. That power is exclusively given to this Court, 
at dapat kayo lang po ang magdesisyon at magpasya kung ano nga ba 'yan.

blocks.source_text:
Mr. Poa. As stated. Your Honors—thank you for the question. As stated last week. Your 
Honors, it is our position of the Defense that that is not obiter dictum, precisely because. Your 
Honors, it is our theory that when the Supreme Court decided the case of Duterte vs. HOR, the 
point that the one-year bar was triggered precisely because there was a violation of due process. 
That’s why, if you read the decision, the Supreme Court, through the words of, of course. Senior 
Associate Justice Marvic Leonen, painstakingly discussed what due process is actually in an 
impeachment proceeding. And in fact, there was a line in that decision that says that they wanted 
to clarify the guidelines on impeachment proceedings.

blocks.normalized_text: further to that your honor hindi long po sa decision pero pati po doon sa binibigay na primer or summary ng supreme court bago nila ilabas iyong buong decision they enumerated the guidelines which were around seven points if i m not mistaken and the sixth point is actually on what is an impeachable offense and in fact we have prepared copies of that primer for the senator-judges which we can distribute so that makita po ninyo na while ang effect ay na-trigger iyong one-year bar it was really due process that the supreme court was discussing in that case to guide yes
blocks.raw_text: Further to that. Your Honor, hindi long po sa decision, pero pati po doon sa binibigay na primer or summary ng Supreme Court bago nila ilabas iyong buong decision, they enumerated the guidelines, which were around seven points, if I’m not mistaken, and the sixth point is actually on what is an impeachable offense. And in fact, we have prepared copies of that primer for the Senator- Judges, which we can distribute, so that makita po ninyo na while ang effect ay na-trigger iyong one-year bar, it was really due process that the Supreme Court was discussing in that case to guide—yes.

blocks.normalized_text: so in the first page sabi dito second considering the nature of the offices and the institutions that are subject to impeachment its effect on the independence of constitutional departments and organs and its nature as a constitutional process all legal issues involving impeachment proceedings are subject to judicial review while the court does not determine when who and whether an impeachable officer may be removed and disqualified from political office it has the duty to construe the process mandated by the constitution
blocks.raw_text: So, in the first page, sabi dito, “Second, considering the nature of the offices and the institutions that are subject to impeachment, its effect on the independence of constitutional departments and organs, and its nature as a constitutional process, all legal issues involving impeachment proceedings, are subject to judicial review. blocks.source_text:
Senator-Judge Cayetano (A). So, in the first page, sabi dito, “Second, considering the nature 
of the offices and the institutions that are subject to impeachment, its effect on the independence 
of constitutional departments and organs, and its nature as a constitutional process, all legal issues 
involving impeachment proceedings, are subject to judicial review. While the Court does not 
determine when, who, and whether an impeachable officer may be removed and disqualified from 
political office, it has the duty to construe the process mandated by the Constitution.”

blocks.normalized_text: first of all your honor i think we must point out one of the plainest reasons why that statement in the duterte ruling is obiter dictum and that simply is because there was no trial yet to speak of when the supreme court rendered that decision paano po magiging issue iyong nangyayari ngayon so trial samantalang noong dinesisyunan po iyan ay wala pa pong trial therefore any statements by the court about how the trial should be conducted are outside the issues of that case and while they may be guidelines they are not binding as judicial precedent to us and to the future generations
blocks.raw_text: First of all. Your Honor, I think we must point out one of the plainest reasons why that statement in the Duterte ruling is obiter dictum. And that simply is because there was no trial yet to speak of when the Supreme Court rendered that decision. Therefore, any statements by the Court about how the trial should be conducted are outside the issues of that case. And while they may be guidelines, they are not binding as judicial precedent to us and to the future generations.
blocks.source_text:
Mr. Diokno. First of all. Your Honor, I think we must point out one of the plainest reasons 
why that statement in the Duterte ruling is obiter dictum. And that simply is because there was 
no trial yet to speak of when the Supreme Court rendered that decision. Paano po magiging issue 
iyong nangyayari ngayon so trial samantalang noong dinesisyunan po iyan ay wala pa pong 
trial? Therefore, any statements by the Court about how the trial should be conducted are outside 
the issues of that case. And while they may be guidelines, they are not binding as judicial 
precedent to us and to the future generations.
