Representative Diokno. For unexplained wealth. Your Honor, I believe the ultimate faets must 
include the following: first, that the person involved amassed wealth; seeond, that that wealth is 
manifestly disproportionate to that person’s lawful income. So, kailangan po natin makita kung 
ano iyong kaniyang kayamanan at kailangan natin ikumpara doon sa lehitimo niyang kinita.

Honorable Court.

criticism ng iba, is this a fishing expedition? Kasi, if I had five companies, and they were all making 
money and supposedly I only own 1% of that company, but I’m getting half of that income, I would 
agree with you, i-subpoena laHat iyan. Tingnan natin iyong flow niyan, baka iyan iyong pumasok

than your monthly sweldo, both of us agree, it’s unexplained. But is it ill-gotten? Or do you need 
more allegations to show that it was ill-gotten?

Representative Diokno. Well, Your Honor, please, the decision whether wealth is unexplained 
or, as you said, ill-gotten is really a decision that this Honorable Court will make based on the 
evidence. Ang gusto lang po namin ay mailatag ang ebidensiya sa inyong Harap para kayo na

Liabilities, and Net Worth, we have to look at the lawful income reported in the ITRs, we have

accounts.

In a court proceeding, whether we’re speaking of an impeachment court or a regular court, the 
judges look at the evidence. They may never be able to know the entire truth, kasi ang pangyayari 
ay nangyari dati pa po. Essentially, a trial is really a reconstruction of something that happened 
before. So, it may happen in some cases that there is enough evidence to make conclusions as far 
as the judgment is concerned, even if the members of the court did not get to see the entire truth.

blocks.source_text:
Senator-Judge Cayetano (A). I don’t think there’s any question that all of us here want na 
mailatag iyong ebidensiya. The question is, do you have the evidence or are you fishing for the 
evidence? And that’s a question not only for you but for every single impeachment process. Not 
only yung umabot dito because myself. Senator Zubiri, Senator Chiz Escudero, then later on, 
Senator Villanueva, has been in the House many times during impeachments of Presidents and 
Chief Justices. But the reason I ask between evidence and proof is because if you have the 
evidence, you want the opportunity, di ba, to bring it here, show the relevancy, coimect it to 
the crime, we admit it so that if it’s true, then, we can convict. The opposite sides na will 
either question; hindi admissible or admissible pero walang relationship iyan, etc.

blocks.raw_text: If I may briefly narrate the procedure that the House might had to undergo for/ the Articles to reach this honorable court. When the complaints were filed before the House of Representatives, the Committee on Justice was tasked not with conducting a trial, not with ferreting out all the evidence, but simply determining whether there was probable cause to proceed with the case. So, hindi po namin hinanap iyong kabuuan ng ebidensiya dahil hindi po namin tungkulin iyon. When we made the determination that there was sufficient probable cause to proceed with that case, then, that is what we submitted in terms of the Articles of Impeachment that reached this Honorable Court.
blocks.source_text:
Representative Diokno. If I may briefly narrate the procedure that the House might had to 
undergo for/ the Articles to reach this honorable court. When the complaints were filed before the 
House of Representatives, the Committee on Justice was tasked not with conducting a trial, not 
with ferreting out all the evidence, but simply determining whether there was probable cause to 
proceed with the case. So, hindi po namin hinanap iyong kabuuan ng ebidensiya dahil hindi po 
namin tungkulin iyon. When we made the determination that there was sufficient probable cause 
to proceed with that case, then, that is what we submitted in terms of the Articles of Impeachment 
that reached this Honorable Court.

blocks.source_text:
So, the Anti-Money Laundering Council does not deal with income—they deal with flow, right? 
So, pag sinabing billion, that doesn’t mean that a certain official has billions in their account. So, 
meaning, if I have a million pesos deposited on January 1, and then on January 5,1 take it out and

blocks.normalized_text: if your honors please the doctrine of piercing the corporate veil applies when we are determining liability of the company or corporation involved in this particular case that doctrine has no application at all we are asking for those records to be produced so that we can determine the extent of participation of the vice president as she herself had admitted those business interests in her saln s kaya po namin hinihingi po iyon kasi sa tingin po namin ay wala pong aplikasyon ang piercing the corporate veil
blocks.raw_text: If Your Honors please, the doctrine of piercing the corporate veil applies when we are determining liability of the company or corporation involved. In this particular case, that doctrine has no application at all. We are asking for those records to be produced so that we can determine the extent of participation of the Vice President as she, herself, had admitted those business interests in her SALN’s kaya po namin hinihingi po iyon. Kasi sa tingin po namin ay wala pong aplikasyon ang piercing the corporate veil.
blocks.source_text:
Representative Diokno. If Your Honors please, the doctrine of piercing the corporate veil 
applies when we are determining liability of the company or corporation involved. In this particular 
case, that doctrine has no application at all. We are asking for those records to be produced so 
that we can determine the extent of participation of the Vice President as she, herself, had admitted 
those business interests in her SALN’s kaya po namin hinihingi po iyon. Kasi sa tingin po namin 
ay wala pong aplikasyon ang piercing the corporate veil.

tecum. These are set forth in Section 3, Rule 21 of the Rules of Court, which provides, among

of the books, documents, or things demanded, which must appear to the Court as prima 
facie relevant.

blocks.normalized_text: settled jurisprudence has affirmed and clarified these requirements in the 2021 case of republic v sandiganbayan dated 2-15-21 the supreme court stated quote a subpoena duces tecum may be issued if the tests of relevancy and definiteness are satisfied the court must ensure that 1 the books documents or other things requested must appear prima facie relevant to the issue subject of the controversy the test of relevancy and 2 such books must be reasonably described by the parties to be readily identified the test of definiteness
blocks.raw_text: Settled jurisprudence has affirmed and clarified these requirements. In the 2021 case of Republic v. Sandiganbayan, dated 2-15-21, the Supreme Court stated, quote, “A subpoena duces tecum may be issued if the tests of relevancy and definiteness are satisfied. The Court must ensure that, ‘(1) the books, documents, or other things requested must appear prima facie relevant to the issue subject of the controversy (the test of relevancy); and, (2) such books must be reasonably described by the parties to be readily identified, the (test of definiteness).’”
blocks.source_text:
Settled jurisprudence has affirmed and clarified these requirements. In the 2021 case of 
Republic v. Sandiganbayan, dated 2-15-21, the Supreme Court stated, quote, “A subpoena duces 
tecum may be issued if the tests of relevancy and definiteness are satisfied. The Court must ensure 
that, ‘(1) the books, documents, or other things requested must appear prima facie relevant to the 
issue subject of the controversy (the test of relevancy); and, (2) such books must be reasonably 
described by the parties to be readily identified, the (test of definiteness).’”

blocks.normalized_text: because philippine civil law legally merges their assets properties and income into a single absolute community it is a legal and mathematical impossibility to determine the respondent s true net worth or lawful income without examining the bank accounts of her husband investigating mr carpio s financial records is therefore not an unwarded intrusion into a third party s privacy but an absolute legal prerequisite in determining the actual aggregated wealth of the respondent
blocks.raw_text: Because Philippine civil law legally merges their assets, properties, and income into a single absolute community, it is a legal and mathematical impossibility to determine the Respondent’s true net worth or lawful income without examining the bank accounts of her husband. Investigating Mr. Carpio’s financial records is therefore not an unwarded intrusion into a third party’s privacy, but an absolute legal prerequisite in determining the actual aggregated wealth of the Respondent.
blocks.source_text:
Because Philippine civil law legally merges their assets, properties, and income into a 
single absolute community, it is a legal and mathematical impossibility to determine the 
Respondent’s true net worth or lawful income without examining the bank accounts of her 
husband. Investigating Mr. Carpio’s financial records is therefore not an unwarded intrusion into 
a third party’s privacy, but an absolute legal prerequisite in determining the actual aggregated 
wealth of the Respondent.

Absent such proof, these entities cannot be subjected to the compulsory process intended for another.

clearly established. Absent such proof, these entities cannot be subjected to the compulsory 
process intended for another.

blocks.normalized_text: as to the request for subpoena against the partnership of carpio lawyers the same is granted with the following reservation in adherence to subido vs pagante certesa mendoza and binay law offices vs court of appeals g r no 216914 the chair notes the practical reality that an examination of a law firm s bank accounts may incidentally disclose sensitive information relating to its clients and legal engagements the court acknowledges that the partnership and or the clients of carpio lawyers may seek appropriate remedies and to challenge compliance with applicable legal requirements particularly in light of the potential legal implications including concerns relating to attorney-client privilege which may also be raised during the trial proper
blocks.raw_text: As to the request for subpoena against the partnership of Carpio lawyers, the same is granted with the following reservation: In adherence to Subido vs. Pagante Certesa Mendoza, and Binay Law Offices vs. Court of Appeals (G.R. No. 216914), the Chair notes the practical reality that an examination of a law firm’s bank accounts may incidentally disclose sensitive information relating to its clients and legal engagements. The Court acknowledges that the partnership and/or the clients of Carpio lawyers may seek appropriate remedies and to challenge compliance with applicable legal requirements particularly in light of the potential legal implications including concerns relating to attorney-client privilege which may also be raised during the trial proper.
blocks.source_text:
As to the request for subpoena against the partnership of Carpio lawyers, the same is granted 
with the following reservation:
In adherence to Subido vs. Pagante Certesa Mendoza, and Binay Law Offices vs. Court of 
Appeals (G.R. No. 216914), the Chair notes the practical reality that an examination of a law firm’s 
bank accounts may incidentally disclose sensitive information relating to its clients and legal 
engagements. The Court acknowledges that the partnership and/or the clients of Carpio lawyers 
may seek appropriate remedies and to challenge compliance with applicable legal requirements 
particularly in light of the potential legal implications including concerns relating to attorney-client 
privilege which may also be raised during the trial proper.

arbitrary disclosures by AMLC personnel. It does not and constitutionally cannot nullify the lawful

blocks.normalized_text: in the landmark case of republic vs sandiganbayan gr no 2322724 the supreme court explicitly rejected the amlc s attempt to quash a subpoena duces tecum ruling verbatim the anti-money laundering council is not merely a repository of reports and information uncovered and suspicious transactions it was created precisely to investigate and institute charges against those suspected to commit money laundering activities the criminal prosecution of such activities would be unduly hampered if we were to prohibit it from disclosing such information for the anti-money laundering council to refuse disclosing the information required of it would be to go against its very function under the law
blocks.raw_text: In the landmark case of Republic vs. Sandiganbayan, (GR No. 2322724), the Supreme Court explicitly rejected the AMLC’s attempt to quash a subpoena duces tecum ruling verbatim — “The Anti-Money Laundering Council is not merely a repository of reports and information uncovered and suspicious transactions. It was created precisely to investigate and institute charges against those suspected to commit money laundering activities. The criminal prosecution of such activities would be unduly hampered if we were to prohibit it from disclosing such information. For the Anti-Money Laundering Council to refuse disclosing the information required of it would be to go against its very function under the law.’’
blocks.source_text:
In the landmark case of Republic vs. Sandiganbayan, (GR No. 2322724), the Supreme Court 
explicitly rejected the AMLC’s attempt to quash a subpoena duces tecum ruling verbatim — 
“The Anti-Money Laundering Council is not merely a repository of reports and information 
uncovered and suspicious transactions. It was created precisely to investigate and institute 
charges against those suspected to commit money laundering activities. The criminal prosecution 
of such activities would be unduly hampered if we were to prohibit it from disclosing such 
information. For the Anti-Money Laundering Council to refuse disclosing the information required 
of it would be to go against its very function under the law.’’

blocks.normalized_text: an administrative foomote or internal intelligence sharing protocol cannot rise to the level of a constitutional or statutory exclusionary rule while the amlc s internal protocols are validly designed to prevent premature leaks during ongoing investigations they cannot legally operate to tie the hands of the impeachment court once the documents are lawfully subpoenaed because the requested suspicious transaction reports and covered transaction reports satisfy the rest of the threshold of prima facie relevance in tracing the alleged accumulation of unexplained wealth under article ii they shall be produced
blocks.raw_text: An administrative foomote or internal intelligence sharing protocol cannot rise to the level of a constitutional or statutory exclusionary rule. While the AMLC’s internal protocols are validly designed to prevent premature leaks during ongoing investigations, they cannot legally operate to tie the hands of the Impeachment Court once the documents are lawfully subpoenaed. Because the requested Suspicious Transaction Reports and Covered Transaction Reports satisfy the rest of the threshold of prima facie relevance in tracing the alleged accumulation of unexplained wealth under Article II, they shall be produced.
blocks.source_text:
An administrative foomote or internal intelligence sharing protocol cannot rise to the level of 
a constitutional or statutory exclusionary rule. While the AMLC’s internal protocols are validly 
designed to prevent premature leaks during ongoing investigations, they cannot legally operate to 
tie the hands of the Impeachment Court once the documents are lawfully subpoenaed. Because the 
requested Suspicious Transaction Reports and Covered Transaction Reports satisfy the rest of the 
threshold of prima facie relevance in tracing the alleged accumulation of unexplained wealth under 
Article II, they shall be produced.

blocks.source_text:
As for the parmership of Carpio Lawyers, Section 1 of Republic Act No. 10365, amending 
Section 3 [(a)(7)] of Republic Act No. 9160, treats as covered persons those—including lawyers— 
who manage client money or assets; manage bank,] savings, and securities accounts; organize 
contributions for companies; or create, operate, or manage juridical persons or arrangements. 
Section 2(c) of AMLC Regulatory Issuance B, No. 1 Series of 2018, adopted on May 10, 2018, and 
filed on June 13, 2018, expressly reiterates this activity-based rule.

blocks.normalized_text: the court recognizes the statutory exception for lawyers acting as independent legal professionals where the information concerns their clients or disclosure would compromise client confidences or the attorney-client relationship absent any showing however that the particular records contained such information or would compromise a protected confidence the exception cannot be invoked merely because the entity concerned is a law paitnership it protects client confidences it does not confer blanket immunity upon every financial record bearing the law firm s name
blocks.raw_text: The Court recognizes the statutory exception for lawyers acting as independent legal professionals where the information concerns their clients or disclosure would compromise client confidences or the attorney-client relationship. Absent any showing, however, that the particular records contained such information or would compromise a protected confidence, the exception cannot be invoked merely because the entity concerned is a law paitnership. It protects client confidences; it does not confer blanket immunity upon every financial record bearing the law firm’s name.
blocks.source_text:
The Court recognizes the statutory exception for lawyers acting as independent legal 
professionals where the information concerns their clients or disclosure would compromise client 
confidences or the attorney-client relationship. Absent any showing, however, that the particular 
records contained such information or would compromise a protected confidence, the exception 
cannot be invoked merely because the entity concerned is a law paitnership. It protects client 
confidences; it does not confer blanket immunity upon every financial record bearing the law 
firm’s name.

blocks.raw_text: After examining the records sought, the Court finds that the requested documents meet the requirements set forth. They are reasonably described, readily identifiable, and prima facie relevant and material to the allegations under Article II. However, the Court denies the request in so far as it seeks the records of JTC Group of Companies, Philippines Inc., and Pikimong Pikimong Philippines Corporation. The records sought, therefore, do not, at this time, satisfy the requirement of prima facie relevance under Rule 21 of the Rules of Civil Procedure.
blocks.source_text:
After examining the records sought, the Court finds that the requested documents meet 
the requirements set forth. They are reasonably described, readily identifiable, and prima facie 
relevant and material to the allegations under Article II. However, the Court denies the request 
in so far as it seeks the records of JTC Group of Companies, Philippines Inc., and Pikimong 
Pikimong Philippines Corporation. The Prosecution failed to establish a prima facie nexus between 
these entities to either the Respondent or to her spouse. The records sought, therefore, do not, 
at this time, satisfy the requirement of prima facie relevance under Rule 21 of the Rules of 
Civil Procedure.

blocks.raw_text: This Court will not allow it. For the same reasons discussed in the Court’s earlier Order, consistent with the same jurisprudence, which are hereby adopted by reference, the Court likewise holds that the requested records are sought to establish theRespondent’s financial baseline and capacity only, not to inquire into or prosecute alleged impeachable acts occurring outside the present Articles of Impeachment. blocks.source_text:
This Court will not allow it. For the same reasons discussed in the Court’s earlier Order, 
consistent with the same jurisprudence, which are hereby adopted by reference, the Court likewise 
holds that the requested records are sought to establish theRespondent’s financial baseline and 
capacity only, not to inquire into or prosecute alleged impeachable acts occurring outside the 
present Articles of Impeachment. Accordingly, these records are required to establish a factual 
baseline against which the Respondent’s assets, financial transactions, and business interests 
during her present term may be assessed.

blocks.normalized_text: we are also not without precedent during the corona impeachment trial the salns prior to his appointment as chief justice in 2010 were presented on the january 18 2012 hearing of the court likewise evidence and testimony concerning properties and transactions before his appointment as associate justice were presented before the court on march 13 2012 therefore the production of records from 2007 to 2021 is therefore allowed and authorized strictly for purposes of establishing a comparative baseline only they shall not be used as proof of another independent impeachable offense their admissibility and evidentiary use shall remain subject to the prosecution establishing their connection to the alleged unexplained wealth during the respondent s present term
blocks.raw_text: We are also not without precedent. During the Corona Impeachment Trial, the SALNs prior to his appointment as Chief Justice in 2010 were presented on the January 18, 2012 hearing of the Court. Likewise evidence and testimony concerning properties and transactions before his appointment as Associate Justice were presented before the Court on March 13, 2012. Therefore, the production of records from 2007 to 2021 is, therefore, allowed and authorized strictly for purposes of establishing a comparative baseline only. They shall not be used as proof of another independent impeachable offense. Their admissibility and evidentiary use shall remain subject to the Prosecution establishing their connection to the alleged unexplained wealth during the Respondent’s present term.
blocks.source_text:
We are also not without precedent. During the Corona Impeachment Trial, the SALNs prior 
to his appointment as Chief Justice in 2010 were presented on the January 18, 2012 hearing of the 
Court. Likewise evidence and testimony concerning properties and transactions before his 
appointment as Associate Justice were presented before the Court on March 13, 2012. Therefore, 
the production of records from 2007 to 2021 is, therefore, allowed and authorized strictly for 
purposes of establishing a comparative baseline only. They shall not be used as proof of another 
independent impeachable offense. Their admissibility and evidentiary use shall remain subject to 
the Prosecution establishing their connection to the alleged unexplained wealth during the 
Respondent’s present term.

blocks.raw_text: The Court is likewise unconvinced by Respondent’s argument that the issuance of the subpoena will usurp the authority of the BIR under Section 5 of the NIRC. This Court is not determining the tax deficiencies or civil liabilities, which remain within the province of the BIR. It is exercising its constitutional mandate to determine public accountability under the Articles of Impeachment. Neither does the pendency of a BIR audit suspend or limit the Senate’s exclusive jurisdiction to try impeachment cases.
blocks.source_text:
The Court is likewise unconvinced by Respondent’s argument that the issuance of the 
subpoena will usurp the authority of the BIR under Section 5 of the NIRC. This Court is not 
determining the tax deficiencies or civil liabilities, which remain within the province of the BIR. 
It is exercising its constitutional mandate to determine public accountability under the Articles of 
Impeachment. Neither does the pendency of a BIR audit suspend or limit the Senate’s exclusive 
jurisdiction to try impeachment cases.

blocks.normalized_text: the respondent invokes section 270 of the national internal revenue code to prevent the production of the requested records the objection is overruled in part the supreme court expressly ruled in dipad v spouses olivan gr 168771 that section 270 of the nirc obviously does not address the confidentiality of itrs it merely penalizes the unauthorized disclosure of taxpayer information except as otherwise provided for by law it does not bar the production of documents
blocks.raw_text: The Respondent invokes Section 270 of the National Internal Revenue Code to prevent the production of the requested records. The objection is overruled, in part. The Supreme Court expressly ruled in Dipad v. Spouses Olivan, GR 168771, that “Section 270 of the NIRC obviously does not address the confidentiality of ITRs.” It merely penalizes the unauthorized disclosure of taxpayer information except as otherwise provided for by law. It does not bar the production of documents.
blocks.source_text:
The Respondent invokes Section 270 of the National Internal Revenue Code to prevent the 
production of the requested records. The objection is overruled, in part. The Supreme Court 
expressly ruled in Dipad v. Spouses Olivan, GR 168771, that “Section 270 of the NIRC obviously 
does not address the confidentiality of ITRs.” It merely penalizes the unauthorized disclosure of 
taxpayer information except as otherwise provided for by law. It does not bar the production of 
documents.

blocks.raw_text: However, the Respondent correctly invokes Section 71, and other provisions of the NIRC, which recognizes only three instances in which a taxpayer’s records may be opened for inspection: No. 1, upon the order of the President or authorization of the President; No. 2, upon the request of a foreign tax authority, with the approval of the President; or No. 3, upon the taxpayer’s waiver.
blocks.source_text:
However, the Respondent correctly invokes Section 71, and other provisions of the NIRC, 
which recognizes only three instances in which a taxpayer’s records may be opened for inspection: 
No. 1, upon the order of the President or authorization of the President; No. 2, upon the request 
of a foreign tax authority, with the approval of the President; or No. 3, upon the taxpayer’s waiver.

Nevertheless, the existence of these statutory limitations does not, at this stage, preclude the 
issuance of the requested subpoena. The issuance of a subpoena is merely a preliminary procedural 
step and does not, by itself, authorize the disclosure, inspection, or admission of the requested 
tax records into evidence. In fact, this issuance is the order that shall set, if at all, things in motion.

materiality, authenticity, admissibility, and other evidentiary infirmities. The issuance of subpoena 
should not be construed as a ruling on the admissibility of the documents ultimately produced.

blocks.normalized_text: senator-judge cayetano a thanked presiding officer escudero for the comprehensive ruling noting that both matters had already been thoroughly discussed during the caucus and proceedings while acknowledging that only two motions were under consideration he stated that he agreed with certain portions of the ruling but disagreed with others by way of example he expressed support for the issuance of subpoenas covering records from 2022 to 2026 and for obtaining the know your client opening balance as a reference point rather than formally objecting during the trial he proposed submitting a written manifestation identifying the portions of the ruling with which he agreed or disagreed and suggested that other senator-judges could do the same if they so wished the written continuing objection of senator-judge cayetano a to the ruling of the presiding officer dated 20 july 2026 is attached as annex a
blocks.raw_text: Senator-Judge Cayetano (A) thanked Presiding Officer Escudero for the comprehensive ruling, noting that both matters had already been thoroughly discussed during the caucus and proceedings. While acknowledging that only two motions were under consideration, he stated that he agreed with certain portions of the ruling but disagreed with others. By way of example, he expressed support for the issuance of subpoenas covering records from 2022 to 2026 and for obtaining the Know Your Client opening balance as a reference point. Rather than formally objecting during the trial, he proposed submitting a written manifestation identifying the portions of the ruling with which he agreed or disagreed, and suggested that other senator-judges could do the same, if they so wished. (The written continuing objection of Senator-Judge Cayetano (A) to the Ruling of the Presiding Officer dated 20 July 2026 is attached as Annex A.)
blocks.source_text:
Senator-Judge Cayetano (A) thanked Presiding Officer Escudero for the comprehensive ruling, 
noting that both matters had already been thoroughly discussed during the caucus and proceedings. 
While acknowledging that only two motions were under consideration, he stated that he agreed 
with certain portions of the ruling but disagreed with others. By way of example, he expressed 
support for the issuance of subpoenas covering records from 2022 to 2026 and for obtaining the 
Know Your Client opening balance as a reference point. Rather than formally objecting during 
the trial, he proposed submitting a written manifestation identifying the portions of the ruling 
with which he agreed or disagreed, and suggested that other senator-judges could do the same, if 
they so wished. (The written continuing objection of Senator-Judge Cayetano (A) to the 
Ruling of the Presiding Officer dated 20 July 2026 is attached as Annex A.)

(1) the phrases “including but not limited to” on page 2 of the request to the BIR for “all records 
filed with, submitted to, or maintained by the BIR for taxable year 2007 to 2025,” and (2) “and all

blocks.raw_text: Presiding Officer Escudero replied that, although such language is commonly used by lawyers, the Court’s interpretation would be limited to the documents specifically identified in the requests. He said the catch-all provisions would therefore be excluded, as the concerned agencies would otherwise be unable to determine which documents to produce. Senator-Judge Cayetano (A) thanked the Presiding Officer and reiterated that he would submit a written manifestation identifying the portions of the ruling which he agreed or disagreed with.
blocks.source_text:
Presiding Officer Escudero replied that, although such language is commonly used by lawyers, 
the Court’s interpretation would be limited to the documents specifically identified in the requests. 
He said the catch-all provisions would therefore be excluded, as the concerned agencies would 
otherwise be unable to determine which documents to produce. Senator-Judge Cayetano (A) 
thanked the Presiding Officer and reiterated that he would submit a written manifestation identifying 
the portions of the ruling which he agreed or disagreed with.

blocks.section_title: EXPLANATIONS OF SENATOR-JUDGES ON THE RULING OF THE PRESIDING OFFICER
blocks.source_text:
By Senator-Judge Padilla
Explaining his stance on the ruling of the Presiding Officer, Senator-Judge Padilla delivered 
the following speech;
Malaga! ko na pong isinusulong ang Freedom of Information dahil ito po ay bumagsak sa 
aking komite. Ito po ay tunay na kailangan natin sapagkat dito po talaga natin mapapatunayan 
iyong sinasabi noting “public trust.” Pero mayroon din po leasing napakahalaga—’yung 
tinatawag na “equal protection of the laws.”

blocks.raw_text: Hindi ko po makakalimutan ang naging pahayag ng dating Senadora Miriam Santiago, dito mismo. Siya po ay isang huwes at ginagalang na propesora ng batas. Ang sabi niya, “Constitutional analysis is a technical field.” Ang pagsusuri sa Konstitusyon ay Hindi isang sining; isa itong teknikal na larangan. Hindi po dapat ito ini-interpret nang basta-basta, o kailangan may due process.

blocks.raw_text: Noong una ko pong narinig kanina, naipaliwanag po ng ating Senator-Judge Alan Peter Cayetano, tinanong sa Prosekusyon kung ganito rin ba ang ginawa nila noong may impeachment kay Presidente Bongbong Marcos. Napakalinaw po na ang sagot nila ay Hindi naman sila nagtanong, Hindi sila nagsaliksik kung paanong ginagawa nila ngayon sa Pangalawang Pangulo. Ang akin po lamang: makatarungan ba na Hindi pareHas ang trato natin dito? Dapat Sana pareHas.

FOI. Kasi kung ganito na parang meron tayong tinititigan sa tinitingnan, Hindi maganda ito 
para sa taumbayan. Tandaan po natin, pantay-pantay tayo sa Harap ng Diyos at ng batas.

Pursuant to Senator-Judge Legarda’s manifestation, following is her explanation on the

We are deciding today whether certain financial records should be compelled for production

blocks.section_title: EXPLANATIONS OF SENATOR-JUDGES ON THE RULING OF THE PRESIDING OFFICER
blocks.source_text:
By Senator-Judge Marcos
following is her explanation on the
Pursuant to Senator-Judge Marcos’s manifestation, 
ruling of the Presiding Officer:
To establish that Vice President Sara Duterte amassed unexplained wealth manifestly 
disproportionate to her lawful income, it must be shown that: 1) she accumulated wealth; and 
2) such wealth caimot be traced to lawful sources.

blocks.normalized_text: bir records may likewise show whether any increase in wealth is supported by declared income or other lawful sources although tax laws contain no express impeachment exception i opine that section 71 of the nirc permits the examination of tax records for an impeachment trial when authorized by the president under appropriate circumstances i would therefore have voted to subpoena them
blocks.raw_text: BIR records may likewise show whether any increase in wealth is supported by declared income or other lawful sources. Although tax laws contain no express impeachment exception, I opine that Section 71 of the NIRC permits the examination of tax records for an impeachment trial when authorized by the President. Under appropriate circumstances, I would therefore have voted to subpoena them.

Let me be clear: this Court may try impeachable offenses—and only impeachable offenses.
It has no jurisdiction to adjudge non-impeachable offenses, however grave. To do so would usurp 
powers belonging to the Judiciary, a co-equal branch of Government.

blocks.normalized_text: nagsimula ang termino ng bise president noong hunyo 30 2022 ang mga sinabing ginawa bago ang petsang ito ay hindi maaaring maging paksa ng impeachment laban sa kanya bilang bise presidente anumang alegasyon ng hindi mapaliwanag na yaman na umano y naipon bago ang hunyo 30 2022 ay wala sa saklaw ng paglilitis sa impeachment kung may usapin tungkol sa mga naipong ari-arian bago ang kanyang panunungkulan bilang bise president ang nararapat na forum para rito ay ang mga regular na hukuman at hindi ang impeachment court
blocks.raw_text: Nagsimula ang termino ng bise president noong Hunyo 30, 2022. Ang mga sinabing ginawa bago ang petsang ito ay hindi maaaring maging paksa ng impeachment laban sa kanya bilang bise presidente. Anumang alegasyon ng hindi mapaliwanag na yaman na umano ’y naipon bago ang Hunyo 30, 2022 ay wala sa saklaw ng paglilitis sa impeachment. Kung may usapin tungkol sa mga naipong ari-arian bago ang kanyang panunungkulan bilang bise president, ang nararapat na forum para rito ay ang mga regular na hukuman at hindi ang Impeachment Court.
blocks.section_title: EXPLANATIONS OF SENATOR-JUDGES ON THE RULING OF THE PRESIDING OFFICER

Nagsimula ang termino ng bise president noong Hunyo 30, 2022. Ang mga sinabing 
ginawa bago ang petsang ito ay hindi maaaring maging paksa ng impeachment laban sa 
kanya bilang bise presidente. Anumang alegasyon ng hindi mapaliwanag na yaman na umano ’y

usapin tungkol sa mga naipong ari-arian bago ang kanyang panunungkulan bilang bise 
president, ang nararapat na forum para rito ay ang mga regular na hukuman at hindi ang

blocks.normalized_text: some may argue that financial records dating to 2007 are sought merely to establish a baseline for determining whether wealth was unexplainedly accumulated this is mistaken since vice president sara became an impeachable officer only on june 30 2022 the relevant baseline is her wealth as of that date
blocks.raw_text: Some may argue that financial records dating to 2007 are sought merely to establish a “baseline” for determining whether wealth was unexplainedly accumulated. This is mistaken. Since Vice President Sara became an impeachable officer only on June 30, 2022, the relevant baseline is her wealth as of that date.
blocks.section_title: EXPLANATIONS OF SENATOR-JUDGES ON THE RULING OF THE PRESIDING OFFICER

Some may argue that financial records dating to 2007 are sought merely to establish a 
“baseline” for determining whether wealth was unexplainedly accumulated. This is mistaken. Since 
Vice President Sara became an impeachable officer only on June 30, 2022, the relevant baseline is 
her wealth as of that date.

blocks.normalized_text: this court cannot perform its constitutional duty while disregarding the constitution fi-om which that duty arises we cannot ignore the ruling of its final arbiter of the constitution and render meaningless the safeguards of our fundamental law to do so would constitute grave abuse of discretion amounting to lack or excess of constitutional jurisdiction
blocks.raw_text: This Court cannot perform its constitutional duty while disregarding the Constitution fi-om which that duty arises. We cannot ignore the ruling of its final arbiter of the Constitution and render meaningless the safeguards of our fundamental law. To do so would constitute grave abuse of discretion amounting to lack or excess of constitutional jurisdiction.

blocks.source_text:
This Court cannot perform its constitutional duty while disregarding the Constitution fi-om 
which that duty arises. We cannot ignore the ruling of its final arbiter of the Constitution and render 
meaningless the safeguards of our fundamental law. To do so would constitute grave abuse of 
discretion amounting to lack or excess of constitutional jurisdiction.

for their issuance.

blocks.normalized_text: on the period covered by the subpoenas senator-judge cayetano p noted that the requested dates covered records from 2007 to 2026 whereas the period relevant to the proceedings covered vice president duterte s term from 2022 to 2026 she acknowledged the jurisprudence cited by the prosecution and relied upon by presiding officer escudero which recognized that earlier records may be examined to establish a financial baseline
blocks.raw_text: On the period covered by the subpoenas, Senator-Judge Cayetano (P) noted that the requested dates covered records from 2007 to 2026, whereas the period relevant to the proceedings covered Vice President Duterte’s term from 2022 to 2026. She acknowledged the jurisprudence cited by the Prosecution and relied upon by Presiding Officer Escudero, which recognized that earlier records may be examined to establish a financial baseline.
blocks.source_text:
On the period covered by the subpoenas, Senator-Judge Cayetano (P) noted that the requested 
dates covered records from 2007 to 2026, whereas the period relevant to the proceedings covered 
Vice President Duterte’s term from 2022 to 2026. She acknowledged the jurisprudence cited by 
the Prosecution and relied upon by Presiding Officer Escudero, which recognized that earlier 
records may be examined to establish a financial baseline.

blocks.normalized_text: nevertheless in the interest of protecting the rights of the person on trial senator-judge cayetano p expressed that view that the inquiry could have initially been limited to records from 2022 to 2026 she reasoned that if those records alone already revealed unexplained matters during the relevant period there might have been no need to examine earlier financial records she viewed this as a reasonable middle ground that would respect the rights of persons brought before the impeachment court while ultimately submitting to the presiding officer s ruling
blocks.raw_text: Nevertheless, in the interest of protecting the rights of the person on trial, Senator-Judge Cayetano (P) expressed that view that the inquiry could have initially been limited to records from 2022 to 2026. She reasoned that if those records alone already revealed unexplained matters during the relevant period, there might have been no need to examine earlier financial records. She viewed this as a reasonable middle ground that would respect the rights of persons brought before the Impeachment Court, while ultimately submitting to the Presiding Officer’s ruling.
blocks.source_text:
Nevertheless, in the interest of protecting the rights of the person on trial, Senator-Judge 
Cayetano (P) expressed that view that the inquiry could have initially been limited to records 
from 2022 to 2026. She reasoned that if those records alone already revealed unexplained 
matters during the relevant period, there might have been no need to examine earlier financial 
records. She viewed this as a reasonable middle ground that would respect the rights of 
persons brought before the Impeachment Court, while ultimately submitting to the Presiding 
Officer’s ruling.

Presiding Officer Escudero stated that the Court has received the Prosecution’s Motion to Set 
a Conference for the Transfer of Markings and Comparison of Video Exhibits. As the motion was 
received on Friday, July 17, 2026, he mentioned that the Court was unable to act upon the dates 
requested therein. Relative thereto, he said that the Court has set the conference the following day, 
July 21, 2026 at 9:00 a.m. and that due notice was being given to both parties for the purpose.

blocks.normalized_text: presiding officer escudero informed the court that he and the senate president had received a letter of manifestation from senator-judge marcoleta requesting that he be allowed to vote citing a rule adopted during the impeachment trial of president estrada allowing a senator who was unable to vote due to unavoidable circumstances to cast a vote on any matter before the court as the senate would resume its regular legislative functions the following week he stated that the matter would be referred to the senate in its legislative capacity as any amendment to the impeachment rules allowing a senator-judge to participate in voting despite unavoidable circumstances falls within the senate s authority
blocks.raw_text: Presiding Officer Escudero informed the Court that he and the Senate President had received a letter of manifestation from Senator-Judge Marcoleta requesting that he be allowed to vote, citing a rule adopted during the Impeachment Trial of President Estrada allowing a senator who was unable to vote due to unavoidable circumstances to cast a vote on any matter before the Court. As the Senate would resume its regular legislative functions the following week, he stated that the matter would be referred to the Senate in its legislative capacity, as any amendment to the Impeachment Rules allowing a Senator-Judge to participate in voting despite unavoidable circumstances falls within the Senate’s authority.
blocks.section_title: LETTER OF MANIFESTATION OF SENATOR-JUDGE MARCOLETA
blocks.source_text:
Presiding Officer Escudero informed the Court that he and the Senate President had received 
a letter of manifestation from Senator-Judge Marcoleta requesting that he be allowed to vote, citing 
a rule adopted during the Impeachment Trial of President Estrada allowing a senator who was 
unable to vote due to unavoidable circumstances to cast a vote on any matter before the Court. 
As the Senate would resume its regular legislative functions the following week, he stated that the 
matter would be referred to the Senate in its legislative capacity, as any amendment to the 
Impeachment Rules allowing a Senator-Judge to participate in voting despite unavoidable 
circumstances falls within the Senate’s authority.

The prosecution must have first identified the ultimate fact 
charged and then related how each requested document tends to prove 
that fact. The prosecution cannot obtain the entire financial histories and 
tax records of respondent, her spouse, private corporations and a 
partnership, examine those documents for any irregularity, and only 
afterward determine the allegations upon which the trial will proceed. It 
is precisely the “general inquisitorial examination” which the Supreme 
Court prohibited in Roco v. Contreras} That converts tlie trial into an 
investigation.

House Committee on Justice when it evaluated the impeachment 
complaints against President Ferdinand R. Marcos, Jr. is materially 
different from that used in respondent’s impeachment. In finding the two 
impeachment complaints against President Marcos insufficient in 
substance, the House Committee on Justice examined not only die 
allegations appearing on the face of the complaints but also delved into 
whether the complaints’ supporting documents had been authenticated.

blocks.raw_text: 13. Section 3, Rule 21 of the Rules of Court requires a subpoena duces tecum to contain a reasonable description of the documents demanded, which must appear to the tribunal to be prima facie relevant, thus; “Section 3. Form and contents. - A subpoena shall state the name of the court and the title of the action or investigation, shall be directed to the person whose attendance is required, and in the case of a subpoena duces tecum, it shall also contain a reasonable description of the books, documents or things demanded which must appear to the court prima facie relevant. (3a, R23)” (Emphasis supplied)

name of the court and the title of the action or investigation, shall 
be directed to the person whose attendance is required, and in 
the case of a subpoena duces tecum, it shall also contain a 
reasonable description of the books, documents or things 
demanded which must appear to the court prima facie 
relevant. (3a, R23)” (Emphasis supplied)

blocks.raw_text: 14. It is well-settled in our jurisprudence that before a subpoena duces tecum may issue, tlie court must be satisfied of two things: (1) relevancy, i.e., the documents must appear, on their face, related to the issue in the case; and (2) definiteness, i.e., they must be described well enough to be readily identified.3 15. The Renewed Request and the Request fail to satisfy the test of definiteness as they cover any bank account “maintained, opened, closed, controlled, or beneficially owned by [respondent] and/or [her husband], whether directly or indirectly...or through any nominee, representative, attomey-in-fact, or authorized signatory,” and then adds “and all other relevant bank documents.” A bank cannot comply with such request without conducting its own investigation to determine what falls within the order. That is the opposite of definiteness because it puts the bank in charge of defining the scope of the subpoena. In the case of Marquez v. Desierto, the Honorable Supreme Court held that the Ombudsman can only examine bank deposit accounts upon complying with the requirement, among others, that the account to be examined is clearly identified and the examination is limited to the account specified.4 16. Moreover, the Renewed Request and Request demand the complete financial and tax records of twenty-two companies without identifying a single account, transaction or figure connecting any of them 1 See Aposto/ V. Liibigtm-Rafiiel, G.R. No. 260640.01 October 2025. 4 G.R. No. 135882,21 June 2001.
blocks.source_text:
14. 
It is well-settled in our jurisprudence that before a subpoena 
duces tecum may issue, tlie court must be satisfied of two things: (1) 
relevancy, i.e., the documents must appear, on their face, related to the 
issue in the case; and (2) definiteness, i.e., they must be described well 
enough to be readily identified.3
15. 
The Renewed Request and the Request fail to satisfy the test of 
definiteness as they cover any bank account “maintained, opened, closed, 
controlled, or beneficially owned by [respondent] and/or [her husband], 
whether directly or indirectly...or through any nominee, representative, 
attomey-in-fact, or authorized signatory,” and then adds “and all other 
relevant bank documents.” A bank cannot comply with such request 
without conducting its own investigation to determine what falls within 
the order. That is the opposite of definiteness because it puts the bank in 
charge of defining the scope of the subpoena. In the case of Marquez v. 
Desierto, the Honorable Supreme Court held that the Ombudsman can 
only examine bank deposit accounts upon complying with the 
requirement, among others, that the account to be examined is clearly 
identified and the examination is limited to the account specified.4
16. 
Moreover, the Renewed Request and Request demand the 
complete financial and tax records of twenty-two companies without 
identifying a single account, transaction or figure connecting any of them
1 See Aposto/ V. Liibigtm-Rafiiel, G.R. No. 260640.01 October 2025. 
4 G.R. No. 135882,21 June 2001.

blocks.normalized_text: 17 as to the corporations the requests fail both tests of definiteness and relevance because they are premised solely on the allegation that the companies merely provide a convenient veil for the billions in unexplained financial transactions but the prosecution did not even make a prima facie case to justify that characterization as drafted the request would include the balances and identities of people who merely share an account with one of the spouses and who are neither impeached nor charged with anything in republic v eugenio jr the supreme court held that such a co-depositor holds a statutory right of her own to the secrecy of the account and has standing to defend it 5 the impeachment court should not issue an order that would most likely trample on the rights of innocent third parties
blocks.raw_text: 17. As to the corporations, the requests fail both tests of definiteness and relevance, because they are premised solely on the allegation that the companies merely provide “a convenient veil for the billions in unexplained financial transactions,” but the Prosecution did not even make a prima facie case to justify that characterization. As drafted, the request would include the balances and identities of people who merely share an account with one of the spouses and who are neither impeached nor charged with anything. In Republic v. Eugenio, Jr., the Supreme Court held that such a co-depositor holds a statutory right of her own to the secrecy of the account, and has standing to defend it.5 The Impeachment Court should not issue an order that would most likely trample on the rights of innocent third parties.
blocks.source_text:
17. 
As to the corporations, the requests fail both tests of definiteness 
and relevance, because they are premised solely on the allegation that the 
companies merely provide “a convenient veil for the billions in 
unexplained financial transactions,” but the Prosecution did not even 
make a prima facie case to justify that characterization. As drafted, the 
request would include the balances and identities of people who merely 
share an account with one of the spouses and who are neither impeached 
nor charged with anything. In Republic v. Eugenio, Jr., the Supreme 
Court held that such a co-depositor holds a statutory right of her own to 
the secrecy of the account, and has standing to defend it.5 The 
Impeachment Court should not issue an order that would most likely 
trample on the rights of innocent third parties.

18. 
The requests amount to an unreasonable inquiry. A demand that 
burdens the custodian of records with determining for himself the scope 
of the subpoena; that broadly asks for every detail of transactions over 
the past 20 years; and that reaches the accounts of persons who are 
neither impeached nor charged is not a reasonable order. It is a general 
warrant that the Bill of Rights frowns upon.

blocks.raw_text: 19. The requests likewise failed to meet the requirement that they should not be “unreasonable or oppressive,” a ground for which the subpoena may be quashed. The requests cover twenty years’ worth of transactions (2006 to 2025) from AMLC, and nineteen years’ wortli of bank and tax records (2007 to 2025). Applied to six banks, two individuals, one partnership, and twenty-two companies, and covering every deposit slip, check, transfer, ledger, journal, and tax returns, the sheer volume is oppressive within the meaning of Section 4, Rule 21 of the Rules of Court, even when the underlying records are relevant in principle.
blocks.source_text:
19. 
The requests likewise failed to meet the requirement that they 
should not be “unreasonable or oppressive,” a ground for which the 
subpoena may be quashed. The requests cover twenty years’ worth of 
transactions (2006 to 2025) from AMLC, and nineteen years’ wortli of 
bank and tax records (2007 to 2025). Applied to six banks, two 
individuals, one partnership, and twenty-two companies, and covering 
every deposit slip, check, transfer, ledger, journal, and tax returns, the 
sheer volume is oppressive within the meaning of Section 4, Rule 21 of 
the Rules of Court, even when the underlying records are relevant in 
principle.

The subpoenaes requested by the prosecution are objectionable because, in both breadth and effect, they would authorize intrusions of even more far-reaching consequence than the “general warrants” prohibited in Stonehill v. Diokno.6 In Stonehill, the evil identified by the Supreme Court was not merely the physical act of searching persons, offices, warehouses and/or residences, but that of placing the sanctity of constitutional rights at the mercy of individuals. blocks.source_text:
21. 
The subpoenaes requested by the prosecution are objectionable 
because, in both breadth and effect, they would authorize intrusions of 
even more far-reaching consequence than the “general warrants” 
prohibited in Stonehill v. Diokno.6 In Stonehill, the evil identified by the 
Supreme Court was not merely the physical act of searching persons, 
offices, warehouses and/or residences, but that of placing the sanctity of 
constitutional rights at the mercy of individuals. That same concern is 
present here.

blocks.raw_text: 22. In practical ternis, the subpoenaes sought here are even more intrusive than the search warrants invalidated in Stonehill. While a search warrant is confined by the constitutional requirements of probable cause, particularity, and specificity, here, the subpoenaes compel banks, the AMLC, and the BIR to disclose records accumulated over years, thereby exposing an entire financial history rather than a definite number of documents and transactions.
blocks.source_text:
22. 
In practical ternis, the subpoenaes sought here are even more 
intrusive than the search warrants invalidated in Stonehill. While a search 
warrant is confined by the constitutional requirements of probable cause, 
particularity, and specificity, here, the subpoenaes compel banks, the 
AMLC, and the BIR to disclose records accumulated over years, thereby 
exposing an entire financial history rather than a definite number of 
documents and transactions.

23. 
That the documents produced pursuant to a subpoena do not ipso 
jure become evidence, unlike property or document seized under a search 
warrant, is beside the point. The invasion of privacy already occurs upon 
disclosure, not only upon the subsequent admission of the documents into 
evidence.

blocks.source_text:
24. 
This is especially acute in an impeachment proceeding which is 
conducted imder severe public scrutiny. Every hearing is broadcast live, 
extensively reported by the media, and subjected to real-time public 
commentary. Hence, even if particular documents are ultimately excluded 
from evidence, the disclosure in itself may irreversibly expose 
confidential information, with the resulting injury no longer possible to 
be undone.

blocks.normalized_text: 25 it should be noted that a ctr from the amlc is triggered by the amount of the transaction more than php 500 000 while an str is triggered by the appearance of an irregularity 7 both reports however only indicate the movement of funds not income the law treats flow and income as two distinct concepts income for tax and for unexplained-wealth purposes is a gain or flow of wealth that accrues to and is realized by the taxpayer and not the mere passage of money through her hands
blocks.raw_text: 25. It should be noted that a CTR from the AMLC is triggered by the amount of the transaction (more than Php 500,000), while an STR is triggered by the appearance of an irregularity.7 Both reports, however, only indicate the movement of funds, not income. The law treats “flow” and “income” as two distinct concepts. Income, for tax and for unexplained-wealth purposes, is a gain or flow of wealth that accrues to and is realized by the taxpayer and not the mere passage of money through her hands.
blocks.source_text:
25. 
It should be noted that a CTR from the AMLC is triggered by 
the amount of the transaction (more than Php 500,000), while an STR is 
triggered by the appearance of an irregularity.7 Both reports, however, 
only indicate the movement of funds, not income. The law treats “flow” 
and “income” as two distinct concepts. Income, for tax and for 
unexplained-wealth purposes, is a gain or flow of wealth that accrues to 
and is realized by the taxpayer and not the mere passage of money 
through her hands.

blocks.source_text:
26. 
A person may move a hundred million pesos through a bank 
account in a year and yet earn nothing from it, while another may earn a 
hundred million and have very little movement. Movements through a 
bank account may include fund transfers between accounts, loan 
proceeds, borrowed funds which were returned, payments received for 
another person, or, plainly, the repeated movement of the same funds. 
Simply adding every transaction together does not establish that the same 
constitutes income or wealth.

blocks.normalized_text: 30 respondent s former positions as vice mayor and mayor of davao city or acts committed as a private citizen are not impeachable offices under the 1987 constitution any offense allegedly committed in those capacities may and where the evidence supports should be investigated and prosecuted under the laws applicable to public officers such acts may be the subject of proceedings before the office of the ombudsman criminal prosecution before regular courts civil forfeiture proceedings under republic act no 1379 prosecution under the national international revenue code as amended among others such acts are not however proper subjects of an impeachment proceeding
blocks.raw_text: 30. Respondent’s former positions as Vice Mayor and Mayor of Davao City, or acts committed as a private citizen, are not impeachable offices under the 1987 Constitution. Any offense allegedly committed in those capacities may, and, where the evidence supports, should be investigated and prosecuted under the laws applicable to public officers. Such acts may be the subject of proceedings before the Office of the Ombudsman, criminal prosecution before regular courts, civil forfeiture proceedings under Republic Act No. 1379, prosecution under the National International Revenue Code, as amended, among others. Such acts are not, however, proper subjects of an impeachment proceeding.
blocks.source_text:
30. 
Respondent’s former positions as Vice Mayor and Mayor of 
Davao City, or acts committed as a private citizen, are not impeachable 
offices under the 1987 Constitution. Any offense allegedly committed in 
those capacities may, and, where the evidence supports, should be 
investigated and prosecuted under the laws applicable to public officers. 
Such acts may be the subject of proceedings before the Office of the 
Ombudsman, criminal prosecution before regular courts, civil forfeiture 
proceedings under Republic Act No. 1379, prosecution under the 
National International Revenue Code, as amended, among others. Such 
acts are not, however, proper subjects of an impeachment proceeding.

blocks.normalized_text: 31 as held in duterte v house of representatives t he basis of any charge for impeachment must be for impeachable acts or omissions committed in relation to their office and during the current term of the impeachable officer in other terms an impeachment is directed only against an impeachable officer s fitness to remain in the particular impeachable office presently held
blocks.raw_text: 31. As held in Duterte v. House of Representatives, “[t]he basis of any charge [for impeachment] must be for impeachable acts or omissions committed in relation to their office and during the current term of the impeachable officer.”* In other terms, an impeachment is directed only against an impeachable officer’s fitness to remain in the particular impeachable office presently held.
blocks.source_text:
31. 
As held in Duterte v. House of Representatives, “[t]he basis of 
any charge [for impeachment] must be for impeachable acts or omissions 
committed in relation to their office and during the current term of the 
impeachable officer.”* In other terms, an impeachment is directed only 
against an impeachable officer’s fitness to remain in the particular 
impeachable office presently held.

blocks.normalized_text: 33 in the case of francisco villanueva jr v court of appeals the honorable supreme court had the occasion to rule that an adjudication on any point within the issues presented by the case cannot be considered as obiter dictum even where the point is only incidentally involved so long as it is presented and decided in the regular course of the consideration of the case and led up to the final conclusion or is a statement as to matter on which the decision is predicated 9 the court further stressed accordingly a point expressly decided does not lose its value as a precedent because the disposition of the case is or might have been made on some other ground or even though by reason of other points in the case the result reached might have been the same if the court had held on the particular point otherwise than it did a decision which the case could have turned on is not regarded as obiter dictum merely because owing to the disposal of the contention it was necessary to consider another question nor can an additional reason in a decision brought forward after the case has been disposed of on one ground be regarded as dicta so also where a case presents two 2 or more points any one of which is sufficient to determine the ultimate issue but the court actually decides all such points the case as an authoritative precedent as to every point decided and none of such points can be regarded as having the status of a dictum and one point should not be denied authority merely because another point was more dwelt on and more fully argued and considered nor does a decision on one proposition make statements of the court regarding other propositions dicta 10 emphases supplied
blocks.raw_text: 33. In the case of Francisco Villanueva Jr. v. Court of Appeals, the Honorable Supreme Court had the occasion to rule that “an adjudication on any point within the issues presented by the case cannot be considered as obiter dictum,” even where the point is “only incidentally involved,” so long as it is “presented and decided in the regular course of the consideration of the case, and led up to the final conclusion,” or “is a statement as to matter on which the decision is predicated.”9 The Court further stressed: “Accordingly, a point expressly decided does not lose its value as a precedent because the disposition of the case is, or might have been, made on some other ground, or even though, by reason of other points in the case, the result reached might have been the same if the court had held, on the particular point, otherwise than it did. A decision which the case could have turned on is not regarded as obiter dictum merely because, owing to the disposal of the contention, it was necessary to consider another question, nor can an additional reason in a decision, brought forward after the case has been disposed of on one ground, be regarded as dicta. So, also, where a case presents two (2) or more points, any one of which is sufficient to determine the ultimate issue, but the court actually decides all such points, the case as an authoritative precedent as to every point decided, and none of such points can be regarded as having the status of a dictum, and one point should not be denied authority merely because another point was more dwelt on and more fully argued and considered, nor does a decision on one proposition make statements of the court regarding other propositions dicta.”10 (Emphases supplied)
blocks.source_text:
33. 
In the case of Francisco Villanueva Jr. v. Court of Appeals, the 
Honorable Supreme Court had the occasion to rule that “an adjudication 
on any point within the issues presented by the case cannot be considered 
as obiter dictum,” even where the point is “only incidentally involved,” 
so long as it is “presented and decided in the regular course of the 
consideration of the case, and led up to the final conclusion,” or “is a 
statement as to matter on which the decision is predicated.”9 The Court 
further stressed:
“Accordingly, a point expressly decided does not lose its value as a 
precedent because the disposition of the case is, or might have 
been, made on some other ground, or even though, by reason of 
other points in the case, the result reached might have been the 
same if the court had held, on the particular point, otherwise than 
it did. A decision which the case could have turned on is not regarded 
as obiter dictum merely because, owing to the disposal of the 
contention, it was necessary to consider another question, nor can an 
additional reason in a decision, brought forward after the case has 
been disposed of on one ground, be regarded as dicta. So, also, where 
a case presents two (2) or more points, any one of which is sufficient 
to determine the ultimate issue, but the court actually decides all such 
points, the case as an authoritative precedent as to every point 
decided, and none of such points can be regarded as having the status 
of a dictum, and one point should not be denied authority merely 
because another point was more dwelt on and more fully argued 
and considered, nor does a decision on one proposition make 
statements of the court regarding other propositions dicta.”10 
(Emphases supplied)

Thus, one cannot limit the proscriptions of due process codified in Duterte to just one mode of impeachment inasmuch as due process applies to all stages of the impeachment process. In other words, one cannot claim that a particular prescription regarding due process is limited to only one particular mode.
blocks.source_text:
35. 
Thus, one cannot limit the proscriptions of due process codified 
in Duterte to just one mode of impeachment inasmuch as due process 
applies to all stages of the impeachment process. In other words, one 
cannot claim that a particular prescription regarding due process is 
limited to only one particular mode.

blocks.raw_text: The Prosecution had the power and duty to gather the necessary evidence before transmitting the Articles of Impeachment 36. Section 2 of Republic Act No. 1405 creates an exception to bank secrecy “in cases of impeacliment.” To be sure, R.A. No. 1405 does not say “during an impeachment trial.” Thus, it bears to reason that the exception encompasses the entire impeachment process, which commences in the House of Representatives.

blocks.normalized_text: 39 the need for a factual baseline cannot be used to circumvent the express ruling of the supreme court in dulerte v house of representatives in fact at this stage of the proceeding it is way past the time for the prosecution to identify a factual baseline that baseline should have been established during proceedings before the house committee on justice before the articles of impeachment were approved by the house of representatives plenary and transmitted to the senate
blocks.raw_text: 39. The need for a “factual baseline” cannot be used to circumvent the express ruling of the Supreme Court in Dulerte v. House of Representatives. In fact, at this stage of the proceeding, it is way past the time for the prosecution to identify a “factual baseline.” That baseline should have been established during proceedings before the House Committee on Justice, before the Articles of Impeachment were approved by the House of Representatives’ plenary and transmitted to the Senate.
blocks.source_text:
39. 
The need for a “factual baseline” cannot be used to circumvent 
the express ruling of the Supreme Court in Dulerte v. House of 
Representatives. In fact, at this stage of the proceeding, it is way past the 
time for the prosecution to identify a “factual baseline.” That baseline 
should have been established during proceedings before the House 
Committee on Justice, before the Articles of Impeachment were approved 
by the House of Representatives’ plenary and transmitted to the Senate.
